title: ZEAL/BZea EH — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
section: Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon peak, by taxon
text:
Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon peak, by taxon


Show code
if (nrow(btpk[sig == "yes"]) == 0) {
  cat("No per-taxon peak clears its threshold; no effect plot.")
} else {
  top <- btpk[sig == "yes"][which.max(`max LOD`)]
  mk <- top$marker
  gm <- pull.geno(cross)[, mk]
  yv <- as.numeric(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))
  ids <- as.character(pull.pheno(cross, "id"))
  ss <- fread(here("data/zeal/samplesheet_3way.csv"))[, .(pedigree, taxon)]
  d <- data.table(
    taxon = ss[match(ids, pedigree), taxon],
    geno = factor(c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")[gm], levels = c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")), y = yv
  )[!is.na(y) & !is.na(geno) & taxon %in% names(pal5)]
  summ <- d[, .(mean = mean(y), se = sd(y) / sqrt(.N), n = .N), by = .(taxon, geno)]
  shown <- summ[n >= 3]
  ncand <- cand[chr == top$chr][which.min(abs(mid - bp_of(mk)))]
  ncand_txt <- if (nrow(ncand)) sprintf("; nearest candidate %s ~%.1f Mb", ncand$symbol, ncand$mid / 1e6) else ""
  ef <- ggplot(d, aes(geno, y, colour = taxon)) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.12, size = 0.5) +
    geom_line(data = shown, aes(y = mean, group = taxon), linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(data = shown, aes(y = mean), size = 2.2) +
    geom_errorbar(data = shown, aes(y = mean, ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.15) +
    geom_text(data = summ, aes(label = n), y = min(d$y), vjust = 0, size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
    facet_wrap(~taxon, nrow = 1) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, guide = "none") +
    labs(
      x = sprintf("genotype at %s (chr%d ~%.1f Mb%s)", mk, top$chr, bp_of(mk) / 1e6, ncand_txt),
      y = sprintf("%s (BLUE)", TRAIT),
      title = sprintf("Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon %s peak — %s, by taxon", TRAIT, mk),
      subtitle = "per-genotype mean ± SE (grey = individual lines; n at base); estimate drawn for classes with n ≥ 3"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
  ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_%s_effect.png", PFX, mk)), ef, width = 10, height = 4.2, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
  print(ef)
}









Read. Effect size and statistical support (LOD/n) are separate. A/A = B73, A/B = het, B/B = homozygous teosinte. Colocalization with a candidate (the “candidate in CI” columns) is suggestive, not proof — the CIs are multi-Mb at this mapping resolution.

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
text:
What this is. A first R/qtl look at ZEAL/BZea DTA — the classic linkage-mapping route as a contrast to the EMMAX-MLM / TASSEL-JLM pipeline in the other ZEAL notebooks. A bcsft cross (BC₂S₃), interval mapping by Haley–Knott, and a 1000-permutation genome-wide threshold. Inputs produced by scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R (TRAIT=DTA).
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(qtl)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
RQ <- here("results/sim/zeal/rqtl")
stopifnot(dir.exists(RQ)) # run: TRAIT=DTA Rscript scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R

# Markers are named S<chr_v5>_<pos_v5> (renamed upstream in zeal_rqtl_scan.R), so a
# peak's v5 position reads straight off its name — no lookup, no v2 confusion.
TRAIT <- "DTA"
trait_lc <- tolower(TRAIT)
bp_of <- function(m) as.integer(sub("^S[0-9]+_", "", m))
ci_str <- function(lbp, hbp) sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", pmin(lbp, hbp) / 1e6, pmax(lbp, hbp) / 1e6)

# flowering-time candidate genes (B73 v5), placed on the TeoNAM cM axis
cand <- fread(here(sprintf("data/teonam/%s_candidate_genes.tsv", trait_lc)))
cand[, mid := (start + end) / 2]
.nat <- fread(here("data/teonam/teonam_v5_native.tsv"))
cand[, cm := mapply(function(c, b) {
  n <- .nat[chr_v5 == c & is.finite(cm)][order(pos_v5)]
  if (nrow(n) < 2) NA_real_ else approx(n$pos_v5, n$cm, xout = b, rule = 2)$y
}, chr, mid)]
# candidate(s) whose gene body falls inside a peak's bp CI
cand_in_ci <- function(ch, lo, hi) {
  s <- cand[chr == ch & mid >= min(lo, hi) & mid <= max(lo, hi), symbol]
  if (length(s)) paste(s, collapse = ", ") else "–"
}
CCT_BP <- cand[symbol == "zmcct10", mid] # for the Zd chr10 effect section below

load_scan <- function(tag) {
  one <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("zeal_dta_scanone_%s.csv", tag)))
  perms <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("zeal_dta_perms_%s.rds", tag)))
  th <- summary(perms, alpha = c(0.05, 0.10))
  list(one = one, thr5 = as.numeric(th[1]), thr10 = as.numeric(th[2]))
}
nc <- load_scan("nocovar")
tx <- load_scan("taxon")

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
section: Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)
text:
Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)
Faceted by chromosome. Blue = scanone + taxon covariate; grey = no covariate; dashed = the 1000-permutation 5% thresholds. Dotted grey verticals mark the flowering-time candidate genes (v5, placed on the cM axis).


Show code
prof <- rbindlist(list(
  cbind(nc$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "no covariate"),
  cbind(tx$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "+ taxon")
))[is.finite(lod)]
prof[, model := factor(model, levels = c("no covariate", "+ taxon"))]
sig <- prof[model == "+ taxon" & lod >= tx$thr5][, .SD[which.max(lod)], by = chr]
pal <- c("no covariate" = "grey65", "+ taxon" = "#2c6fbb")
ytop <- max(prof$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
cl <- cand[chr %in% unique(prof$chr) & is.finite(cm)] # flowering candidates on the cM axis
p <- ggplot(prof, aes(pos, lod, colour = model)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = nc$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["no covariate"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = tx$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["+ taxon"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_point(data = sig, colour = "red", size = 1.6) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop * 1.45) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD",
    title = "ZEAL/BZea DTA — R/qtl (bcsft) scanone, Haley–Knott (TeoNAM JLM grid)",
    subtitle = sprintf(
      "%s markers · 1000-perm 5%% threshold: no-cov = %.2f, +taxon = %.2f · dotted = flowering candidates",
      format(NMARK, big.mark = ","), nc$thr5, tx$thr5
    )
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, "zeal_dta_lod_profile.png"), p, width = 11, height = 4.4, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
p

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
text:
Significant peaks (taxon model, α = 0.05)
R/qtl’s summary() reports at most one peak per chromosome. That is the table below — position and the 1.5-LOD confidence interval in v5 Mb, plus any flowering candidate whose gene body falls inside.


Show code
cipk <- fread(file.path(RQ, "zeal_dta_peaks_ci_taxon.csv"))
knitr::kable(
  cipk[order(chr, -lod), .(
    chr,
    `pos (cM)` = round(pos, 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker) / 1e6, 1),
    LOD = round(lod, 2), pval, marker,
    `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = ci_str(ci_left, ci_right),
    `candidate in CI` = mapply(cand_in_ci, chr, ci_left, ci_right)
  )],
  caption = "Genome-wide significant QTL, taxon model (1000-perm threshold). One peak per chromosome (R/qtl summary), with the 1.5-LOD interval in v5 Mb and any flowering candidate whose gene body falls inside."
)

Multi-peak refinement (airmine refine_peaks)
A single chromosome can carry more than one DTA QTL. To resolve them, scripts/detect_peaks.R (ported from the airmine QTL pipeline) Akima-interpolates each significant chromosome’s LOD curve (10×), runs pracma::findpeaks for up to 5 sub-peaks above the permutation threshold, takes each sub-peak’s 1.5-LOD-drop interval, and resolves overlapping sub-peaks into a maximal set of non-overlapping QTL via an interval graph (igraph independent vertex sets). Positions and the 1.5-LOD-drop confidence intervals are shown in v5 Mb (markers are v5-named, so pos (Mb) reads off the name), with the candidate-in-CI flag. Peak splitting from genotyping noise is handled before the scan, not here: the hard RTIGER/projected calls carry het/hom-teosinte miscalls that punch artificial LOD dives (which would split a QTL and clip its CI). calc.genoprob runs the R/qtl HMM with a realistic error.prob so those calls are down-weighted, and the scans use the resulting genotype probabilities (Haley–Knott) / expected dosage (per-taxon) rather than raw calls — so the LOD curve fed to peak detection is already error-corrected.


Show code
refined <- function(tag) {
  r <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("zeal_dta_peaks_refined_%s.csv", tag)))
  if (nrow(r) == 0) {
    return(r)
  }
  r[order(chr, -lod), .(
    chr,
    `pos (cM)` = round(pos, 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker) / 1e6, 1),
    LOD = round(lod, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thresh, 2), marker,
    `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = ci_str(ci_left, ci_right),
    `candidate in CI` = mapply(cand_in_ci, chr, ci_left, ci_right)
  )]
}
rt <- refined("taxon")
if (nrow(rt) == 0) {
  cat("No refined peaks (no chromosome exceeded the permutation threshold).")
} else {
  knitr::kable(rt, caption = sprintf(
    "Refined multi-peak QTL, taxon model (%d QTL across %d chromosomes) vs %d one-per-chr peaks above. Multiple rows on one chromosome = linked QTL split by the interval-graph deconvolution.",
    nrow(rt), uniqueN(rt$chr), nrow(peakrow("taxon"))
  ))
}

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
text:
Per-taxon (family-resolved), additive-only with QC
Each taxon’s NILs are scanned separately (cf. the Andosol bcfst_by_donor.R approach), with QC to keep low-evidence peaks from surfacing in the small per-family samples (n ≈ 60–380):

Phenotype cleaning is upstream: the DTA BLUEs come from raw plots already cleaned by a per-field×taxa upper Tukey fence (Q3 + 3·IQR, late tail only) applied before SpATS in zeal_spats_blues.R — so the very-late plots are removed at source, not re-fenced here.
Additive-only model on HMM expected dosage — LOD from lm(DTA ~ E[teosinte dosage]), 1 df, where E[g] = P(het) + 2·P(hom-teo) comes from the error-aware calc.genoprob (so het/hom miscalls are down-weighted before the scan, not smoothed afterwards); no dominance term, so a near-empty homozygous-teosinte class cannot make the fit rank-deficient. QC class-counts below use the hard calls.
Marker filters: (a) fraction introgressed (HET or ALT) > 0.05 (HET or ALT because the caller calls het over hom-ALT — BC₂S₃ expectation is ~10.9% hom-teosinte vs ~3.1% het); (b) ≥ 3 samples in every present genotype class (empty classes are fine under an additive model — they carry no parameter).



Show code
bt <- fread(file.path(RQ, "zeal_dta_scanone_by_taxon.csv"))[is.finite(lod)]
pal5 <- c(Zd = "#E41A1C", Zh = "#377EB8", Zl = "#4DAF4A", Zv = "#984EA3", Zx = "#FF7F00")
ytop_bt <- max(bt$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
pt <- ggplot(bt, aes(pos, lod, colour = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop_bt * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop_bt * 1.45) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(
    data = unique(bt[, .(taxon, thr5)]), aes(yintercept = thr5, colour = taxon),
    linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, name = "taxon") +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD (additive)",
    title = "ZEAL/BZea DTA — per-taxon additive scan (TeoNAM JLM grid)",
    subtitle = "additive-only; per-family filters: frac introgressed (HET or ALT) > 0.05 & ≥3 per present class · dotted = candidate genes"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, "zeal_dta_lod_by_taxon.png"), pt, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
pt











Show code
btpk <- bt[,
  {
    i <- which.max(lod)
    ch <- chr[i]
    L <- lod[i]
    ins <- .SD[chr == ch][lod >= L - 1.5]
    lbp <- min(bp_of(ins$marker))
    hbp <- max(bp_of(ins$marker))
    .(
      chr = ch, `pos (cM)` = round(pos[i], 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker[i]) / 1e6, 1),
      marker = marker[i], `max LOD` = round(L, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thr5[1], 2),
      sig = fifelse(L > thr5[1], "yes", "no"),
      `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", lbp / 1e6, hbp / 1e6),
      `candidate in CI` = cand_in_ci(ch, lbp, hbp)
    )
  },
  by = taxon
][order(-`max LOD`)]
knitr::kable(btpk, caption = "Genome-wide additive peak per taxon (filtered: frac introgressed > 0.05 & ≥3 per present class), with the per-taxon 1000-permutation 5% threshold. 1.5-LOD interval in v5 Mb and any flowering candidate inside. 'sig' = the family's peak exceeds its own threshold. One peak per taxon.")

Per-taxon multi-peak refinement
The same find_subpeaks deconvolution applied to each family’s LOD profile — so a taxon carrying two QTL on one chromosome (or across the genome) shows all of them, not just its single global maximum. Thresholded at each family’s own per-taxon permutation 5% (thr5).

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
section: Allele effect at the Zd chr10 peak (near ZmCCT10), by taxon
text:
Allele effect at the Zd chr10 peak (near ZmCCT10), by taxon


Show code
# derive the effect-plot marker from the current Zd per-family peak (no hardcoding,
# so this cannot drift from the peaks table above)
mk <- bt[taxon == "Zd"][which.max(lod), marker]
zd_lod <- bt[taxon == "Zd"][which.max(lod), round(lod, 2)]
zd_mb <- round(bp_of(mk) / 1e6, 1)
zd_dist <- round(abs(bp_of(mk) - CCT_BP) / 1e6, 1)
# 1.5-LOD interval (v5 Mb) for the Zd chr10 peak + ZmCCT10 containment
.ins <- bt[taxon == "Zd" & chr == 10][lod >= max(lod) - 1.5]
zd_ci_lo <- round(min(bp_of(.ins$marker)) / 1e6, 1)
zd_ci_hi <- round(max(bp_of(.ins$marker)) / 1e6, 1)
zd_cct_in <- CCT_BP >= min(bp_of(.ins$marker)) & CCT_BP <= max(bp_of(.ins$marker))
.g <- pull.geno(cross)[, mk]
.d <- as.numeric(pull.pheno(cross, "DTA"))
.ss <- unique(fread(here("data/zeal/samplesheet_3way.csv"))[, .(pedigree, taxon)], by = "pedigree")
.tx <- .ss[match(as.character(pull.pheno(cross, "id")), pedigree), taxon]
.k <- .tx == "Zd" & is.finite(.d) & !is.na(.g)
zd_ador <- round(coef(lm(.d[.k] ~ c(0, 1, 2)[.g[.k]]))[2], 2)
zd_nbb <- sum(.g[.k] == 3)
zd_bbaa <- round(mean(.d[.k][.g[.k] == 3]) - mean(.d[.k][.g[.k] == 1]), 1)



Show code
cat(sprintf(
  paste0(
    "The Zd per-family DTA peak, `%s` (chr10 ≈ %s Mb), lies **~%s Mb from ZmCCT10** ",
    "(96.2 Mb, Chen's DTA10.1), and its **1.5-LOD interval (%s–%s Mb) %s ZmCCT10** — so the ",
    "gene %s. In Zd it is a **large-effect, teosinte-late QTL**: **%+.2f days per teosinte allele, ",
    "%+.1f days B/B−A/A** (n_BB = %d), LOD %s.\n"
  ),
  mk, zd_mb, zd_dist, zd_ci_lo, zd_ci_hi,
  if (zd_cct_in) "includes" else "does not include",
  if (zd_cct_in) "sits inside the support interval" else "falls just outside the support interval",
  zd_ador, zd_bbaa, zd_nbb, zd_lod
))

The Zd per-family DTA peak, S10_100917157 (chr10 ≈ 100.9 Mb), lies ~4.7 Mb from ZmCCT10 (96.2 Mb, Chen’s DTA10.1), and its 1.5-LOD interval (76.8–104.6 Mb) includes ZmCCT10 — so the gene sits inside the support interval. In Zd it is a large-effect, teosinte-late QTL: +1.73 days per teosinte allele, +4.2 days B/B−A/A (n_BB = 12), LOD 5.87.
In maize DTA a shift of a few days is a big effect — the scale of the major cloned flowering genes; the LOD is the statistical support, separate from the effect size. Effect by taxon below: all families shown, but the mean ± SE is drawn only for genotype classes with ≥ 3 samples — undersupported classes (e.g. Zh/Zl A/B, n = 2) show raw points + count, no estimate. Lines beyond the per-taxa 3·IQR upper fence were dropped upstream. A/A = B73, A/B = het, B/B = homozygous teosinte.


Show code
gm <- pull.geno(cross)[, mk] # mk from the zd-setup chunk (current Zd peak)
dta <- as.numeric(pull.pheno(cross, "DTA")) # DTA > 100 already dropped upstream
ids <- as.character(pull.pheno(cross, "id"))
ss <- fread(here("data/zeal/samplesheet_3way.csv"))[, .(pedigree, taxon)]
d <- data.table(
  taxon = ss[match(ids, pedigree), taxon],
  geno = factor(c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")[gm], levels = c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")), dta
)[!is.na(dta) & !is.na(geno) & taxon %in% c("Zd", "Zh", "Zl", "Zv", "Zx")]
summ <- d[, .(mean = mean(dta), se = sd(dta) / sqrt(.N), n = .N), by = .(taxon, geno)]
shown <- summ[n >= 3] # estimate drawn only for classes with >= 3 samples
ef <- ggplot(d, aes(geno, dta, colour = taxon)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.12, size = 0.5) +
  geom_line(data = shown, aes(y = mean, group = taxon), linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(data = shown, aes(y = mean), size = 2.2) +
  geom_errorbar(data = shown, aes(y = mean, ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.15) +
  geom_text(data = summ, aes(label = n), y = min(d$dta), vjust = 0, size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~taxon, nrow = 1) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    x = sprintf("genotype at %s (chr10 ~%s Mb, ~%s Mb from ZmCCT10)", mk, zd_mb, zd_dist), y = "DTA (days)",
    title = sprintf("Allele effect at the Zd chr10 peak — %s, by taxon", mk),
    subtitle = "per-genotype mean DTA ± SE (grey = individual lines; n at base)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("zeal_dta_%s_effect.png", mk)), ef, width = 10, height = 4.2, dpi = 200, bg = "white")

Read. Effect size and statistical support are separate — read effects in days, LOD/n as the support. In maize a DTA shift of a few days is a large effect, the scale of the major cloned flowering genes.
Cleaning the DTA BLUEs (per-taxa fence, upstream) drops the earlier chr1 ~254.7 Mb peak — it was over an uncharacterized universal stress protein and outlier-sensitive — and resolves two pooled peaks (+taxon):

chr10 101.4 Mb (S10_101418767, LOD 11.28) — teosinte-late, +3.9 days B/B−A/A (+1.08 d/allele), n_BB = 28. Large effect and strongly supported: the point estimate is 5.2 Mb distal to ZmCCT10 (Chen’s DTA10.1), but its 1.5-LOD CI (93.2–102.1 Mb) spans ZmCCT10. This is the headline the chr1/USP artifact was masking.
chr9 12.4 Mb (S9_12409400, LOD 8.09) — teosinte-early, −1.0 day (−0.95 d/allele), n_BB = 31; no known flowering gene nearby.

Per-family (additive, now with a per-family 1000-perm 5% threshold ~3.5–4.0): four of five families clear their own threshold. Zd chr10 96.9 Mb (S10_96943345): LOD 8.19 vs thr 3.96 — +6.5 days B/B−A/A (+2.35 d/allele), n_BB = 8, a big teosinte-late effect whose point estimate is 0.7 Mb from ZmCCT10 and whose 1.5-LOD CI (95.9–100.9 Mb) includes the gene, echoing the pooled chr10 peak — the clearest per-family QTL. Zl chr9 94.3 Mb (S9_94322165): LOD 6.83 vs thr 3.52, −6.7 days (−2.97 d/allele) — significant but on only n_BB = 3, so a large effect on thin support. Zx chr9 4.5 Mb (LOD 4.67 vs 3.73, −2.6 d) and Zv chr3 230.3 Mb (LOD 4.23 vs 3.98, +3.4 d) just clear; Zh does not (2.48 < 3.92).
So after honest phenotype cleaning the ZEAL DTA scan recovers a large-effect, well-supported, teosinte-late QTL in the ZmCCT10 region — pooled and in Zd — consistent with TeoNAM’s DTA10.1, plus a chr9 teosinte-early locus. These are not “modest” effects; they are large, and the chr10 one is well supported.

title: ZEAL/BZea Stem-Pigment (StPi) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. The stem-pigment (StPi) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-snp50k, built from the same real BZea pipeline: field StPi → per-genotype empirical logit → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa. StPi is a binary 0/1 stem-pigment call per plot (pigmented / not); the per-genotype phenotype is the proportion of pigmented plots, k/n, and only ~22% of genotypes are ever pigmented.
Trait context. Stem pigmentation is an anthocyanin trait. In TeoNAM (Chen 2019) the pigment/color traits (e.g. glume color) had simple genetic architectures — a single large-effect QTL plus a few small-effect ones — unlike the polygenic flowering (DTA/DTS) or height (PH) traits. The candidates here are the classic maize anthocyanin-biosynthesis genes: the regulators r1/b1 (bHLH) and c1/pl1 (MYB) that switch the pathway on, and the structural enzymes c2 (CHS), chi1 (CHI), pr1 (F3’H), a1 (DFR), a2 (ANS), bz1 (UFGT) and bz2 (GST).
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/stpi_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/stpi_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/stpi_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/stpi_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "STPI"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ZEAL/BZea Stem-Pigment (StPi) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize anthocyanin candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). pathway records the biosynthetic role (regulatory bHLH/MYB vs structural enzyme).


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/stpi_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("StPi candidates (B73 v5, anthocyanin pathway): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)

text:
OLS → MLM: structure control on a pigment trait
The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD); K deflates λ toward 1.

text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype.


Show code

title: ZEAL/BZea Stem-Pigment (StPi) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Multi-caller StPi scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller StPi scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same StPi MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth. The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below). Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (StPi) changes.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/stpi_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/stpi_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "StPi MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the anthocyanin candidate windows.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaf-Greenness SPAD (20 DAS) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. One of three SPAD notebooks. SPAD leaf greenness was measured at two dates (20 and 36 days after sowing). This notebook maps the 20DAS reading; its siblings map the 36DAS reading (spad36das) and a single spatially-corrected SPAD BLUE (spad). Same SNP50K ancestry pipeline → RTIGER mosaic → K → JLM + MLM.
Trait context. SPAD is a leaf-chlorophyll proxy. The candidate set spans the maize chlorophyll pathway and its regulators: biosynthesis (chlh1, oy1, gtr3, urod, por1/2), catabolism / stay-green (pph, nye1/2, sgrl1, lls1, nyc1), light-harvesting (lhcb10, cab1/2), and regulators (nactf132, GOLDEN2-LIKE g2/glk1). The model-independent JLM is the primary QTL tool.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R"))
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/spad20das_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/spad20das_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/spad20das_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/spad20das_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "SPAD20DAS"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaf-Greenness SPAD (20 DAS) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/spad20das_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("SPAD (20DAS) candidates (B73 v5 maize chlorophyll panel): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)

section: Multi-caller SPAD 20DAS scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller SPAD 20DAS scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/spad20das_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/spad20das_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "SPAD 20DAS MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the chlorophyll candidate windows.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Prolificacy (Prolif) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. The prolificacy (Prolif) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-en-mlm-snp50k, built from the same real BZea pipeline end-to-end: field Prolif → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa. Prolif is prolificacy — the propensity to bear more than one ear — and the per-genotype value is the per-field SpATS mean across the two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5). It shares the ear-number candidate panel with the ear-number (EN) notebook; only the phenotype changes.
Trait context. Prolificacy — bearing multiple ears rather than a single dominant ear — is the domestication axis most directly opposed between teosinte and B73: teosinte’s long, branched lateral architecture produces many ears, while the maize apical-dominance program concentrates resources into one or two. Prolif and ear number (EN) are two scorings of the same underlying axillary-meristem-fate biology and share the candidate panel: the core regulators gt1 (grassy tillers1, the prol1.1 QTL gene — teosinte allele derepresses axillary ear buds → prolific ears), tb1 (teosinte branched1, the Hopscotch-driven apical-dominance domestication locus), and tru1 (tassels replace upper ears1, a tb1 target for ear-vs-tassel identity); plus the second-tier inflorescence-architecture / meristem / KRN genes (ub2/ub3, ra1/ra2/ra3, fea2/fea3/td1). Because teosinte ancestry is broadly prolificacy-increasing and partly collinear with K, the mixed model absorbs ancestry-correlated variance, and the model-independent JLM (nested-in-family stepwise) is the primary QTL tool — with gt1/tb1 the two loci most likely to surface as headline, effect-bearing QTL.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "PROLIF"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ZEAL/BZea Prolificacy (Prolif) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize ear-number / prolificacy candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). Candidates are the curated cloned ear-number / prolificacy panel (core gt1/tb1/tru1 + inflorescence-architecture branching/meristem/KRN genes; v5 coordinates); pathway records the tier and protein/role each acts in.


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/prolif_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("PROLIF(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("Prolif candidates (B73 v5; curated maize ear-number / prolificacy panel): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)

title: ZEAL/BZea Prolificacy (Prolif) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
OLS → MLM: structure control
The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD, unfixable by any fixed factor); K deflates λ toward 1 and removes the ancestry-correlated bulk that a near-isogenic, teosinte-ancestry-structured prolificacy panel carries.


Show code
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea Prolif — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_ols,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)









The OLS Manhattan (no K) is genome-wide inflated (ancestry-block LD) — the same structure artifact the MLM K term removes below.


Show code
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n > perm 5%` = c(nsig(ols$P, thr_ols), nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan's 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.")



OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan’s 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.

}

title: ZEAL/BZea Prolificacy (Prolif) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)
Prolif QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, entry by a 1000-permutation empirical α = 0.05 (Chen-style genome-wide FWER; per-trait)). JLM QTL are selected by the stepwise enter/exit P (a model-selection gate), independent of the per-scan permutation FWER line used for the GWAS Manhattans below. Model-independent — the primary Prolif analysis, as in Chen 2019.


Show code
p_lolli <- plot_lollipop(
  here("results/sim/zeal/tassel/prolif_jlm_native1.txt"), mlm,
  "ZEAL/BZea Prolif — JLM QTL genomic distribution (nested in taxon)",
  overlap_csv = overlap,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_jlm_lollipop_snp50k.png")
)
p_lolli

title: ZEAL/BZea Prolificacy (Prolif) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated — the 70%-missing per-SNP panel at 0.4× is sparse — but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing, and the demonstration that ancestry inference is mandatory at low coverage — identical in mechanism to the DTA/PH/EH/EN notebooks.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("Prolif — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("Prolif — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

title: ZEAL/BZea Prolificacy (Prolif) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Multi-caller Prolif scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller Prolif scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same Prolif MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth (not a coverage sweep). The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below). Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (Prolif) changes.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/prolif_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
# best (max -log10P) Prolif candidate window per caller
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "Prolif MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the ear-number / prolificacy candidate windows.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Brace-Root Number (NBR) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. The brace-root-number (NBR) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-en-mlm-snp50k, built from the same real BZea pipeline end-to-end: field NBR → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa. NBR is the number of brace roots — the above-ground shoot-borne (nodal) roots — and the per-genotype value is the per-field SpATS mean across the two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5). Only the phenotype and the candidate panel change vs the EN notebook.
Trait context. Brace roots are the shoot-borne (nodal) roots that emerge from above-ground stem nodes; they set anchorage and lodging resistance and contribute to late-season water/nutrient uptake. The candidate panel is the curated maize brace-/shoot-borne-root cloned-gene set, organized by role: Tier 1 — direct brace-root regulators in the auxin / ARF / AP2 axis (ZmARF11, ZmARF26 downstream of the miR390–TAS3 module — up-regulation increases brace-root length & whorl number; ZmRAP2.7/vgt1, a miR172-target AP2/ERF whose mutant decreases brace-root number; and the auxin-biosynthesis YUCCAs ZmYUC2/ZmYUC4, which set brace-root angle rather than number); Tier 2 — shoot-borne root initiation / emergence (rtcs1 the LBD master regulator of shoot-borne root initiation, its paralog rtcl1, bige1 and vp8 whose mutants increase whorl number, and argos8 which blocks nodal-root emergence); and Tier 3 — lateral-root / modifier Aux/IAA genes (rum1, rul1, lrp1, lrt1) that act mainly on embryonic/lateral roots and are included as auxin-pathway modifiers.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/nbr_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/nbr_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/nbr_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/nbr_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "NBR"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ZEAL/BZea Brace-Root Number (NBR) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize brace-/shoot-borne-root candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). Candidates are the curated cloned brace-root panel (auxin/ARF/AP2 regulators + shoot-borne root initiation/emergence + lateral-root modifiers; v5 coordinates); pathway records the tier and phenotype effect each acts through.


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/nbr_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("NBR(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("NBR candidates (B73 v5; curated maize brace-/shoot-borne-root panel): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)

title: ZEAL/BZea Brace-Root Number (NBR) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
OLS → MLM: structure control
The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD, unfixable by any fixed factor); K deflates λ toward 1 and removes the ancestry-correlated bulk that a near-isogenic, teosinte-ancestry-structured root-architecture panel carries.


Show code
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea NBR — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_ols,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/nbr_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)









The OLS Manhattan (no K) is genome-wide inflated (ancestry-block LD) — the same structure artifact the MLM K term removes below.


Show code
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n > perm 5%` = c(nsig(ols$P, thr_ols), nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan's 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Brace-Root Number (NBR) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)
NBR QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, entry by a 1000-permutation empirical α = 0.05 (Chen-style genome-wide FWER; per-trait)). JLM QTL are selected by the stepwise enter/exit P (a model-selection gate), independent of the per-scan permutation FWER line used for the GWAS Manhattans below. Model-independent — the primary NBR analysis, as in Chen 2019.


Show code
p_lolli <- plot_lollipop(
  here("results/sim/zeal/tassel/nbr_jlm_native1.txt"), mlm,
  "ZEAL/BZea NBR — JLM QTL genomic distribution (nested in taxon)",
  overlap_csv = overlap,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/nbr_jlm_lollipop_snp50k.png")
)
p_lolli

title: ZEAL/BZea Brace-Root Number (NBR) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated — the 70%-missing per-SNP panel at 0.4× is sparse — but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing, and the demonstration that ancestry inference is mandatory at low coverage — identical in mechanism to the DTA/PH/EH/EN notebooks.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("NBR — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("NBR — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/nbr_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

title: ZEAL/BZea Brace-Root Number (NBR) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Multi-caller NBR scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller NBR scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same NBR MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth (not a coverage sweep). The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below). Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (NBR) changes.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/nbr_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
# best (max -log10P) NBR candidate window per caller
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/nbr_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "NBR MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the brace-root candidate windows.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Kinki (zigzag culm) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. The Kinki (zigzag-culm) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-stpi-mlm-snp50k, built from the same real BZea pipeline: field Kinki → per-genotype empirical logit → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa. Kinki is the classic maize “zigzag culm” phenotype (Eyster 1920, J Hered 11:349): dwarfed plants with shortened, thickened, arching ear-region internodes that give the stalk a kinked, zigzag appearance. It was scored CLY23 only as an ordinal 0/1/2 severity, binarized here to “kinked” (score ≥ 1); the per-genotype phenotype is the proportion of kinked plots, k/n.
Trait context. The zigzag culm is a culm / shoot-meristem-architecture defect: shortened, mis-oriented internodes and kinked stalks. The most direct a-priori candidate is gn1/knox4 (Gnarley1) — a dominant KNOX-homeobox mutant that produces curving/twisting culms with short internodes, and the intended comparison gene for this phenotype. The candidate set assembles the loci most likely to underlie a kinked-culm phenotype: the KNOX / KNOX-regulatory axis (gn1, lg3, and the KNOX-repressor rs2, whose recessive loss gives compressed/twisted culms via ectopic KNOX), GA / dwarf internode genes (an1, d9, ct2), CLV–WUS meristem-size receptors (fea2, td1), auxin / inflorescence-meristem genes (bif2, ba1, spi1, ra1), cell-division-plane control (tan1), the cytokinin receptor hsf1, and pigment/linkage anchors for the classical zg1 interval on chr 6L (pl1, sm1) plus the chr3 anchor a1. Because teosinte ancestry is partly collinear with K, the mixed model absorbs ancestry-correlated variance, and the model-independent JLM (nested-in-family stepwise) is the primary QTL tool.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/kinki_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/kinki_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/kinki_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/kinki_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "KINKI"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ZEAL/BZea Kinki (zigzag culm) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize culm/meristem-architecture candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). pathway records the biological role each locus acts through (KNOX / GA-dwarf / meristem / anchor).


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/kinki_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("Kinki candidates (B73 v5; culm/meristem-architecture loci): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)

title: ZEAL/BZea Kinki (zigzag culm) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
OLS → MLM: structure control
The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD); K deflates λ toward 1.


Show code
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea Kinki — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_ols,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/kinki_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)











Show code
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n > perm 5%` = c(nsig(ols$P, thr_ols), nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan's 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Kinki (zigzag culm) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("Kinki — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("Kinki — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/kinki_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

title: ZEAL/BZea Kinki (zigzag culm) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Multi-caller Kinki scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller Kinki scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same Kinki MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth. The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below). Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (Kinki) changes.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/kinki_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/kinki_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "Kinki MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the culm/meristem candidate windows.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Ear-Height (EH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. The ear-height (EH) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-ph-mlm-snp50k, built from the same real BZea pipeline end-to-end: field EH → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa. EH is ear height — measured from the base of the plant to the primary ear-bearing node — and the per-genotype value is the per-field SpATS mean across the two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5). Only the phenotype changes vs the PH notebook.
Trait context. Ear height is a plant-architecture trait tightly correlated with plant height: the ear-bearing node’s position is set by the same internode-elongation program, so EH and PH share genetic control and, in TeoNAM (Chen 2019), a similarly polygenic architecture with few small-effect QTL. The candidate set is a curated panel of cloned maize plant-architecture / height genes spanning the hormone pathways that set internode length: auxin transport/ biosynthesis (br2, ZmPIN1a, spi1), gibberellin (GA) biosynthesis and signaling (an1, d1, d3; DELLA d8, d9), brassinosteroid (BR) biosynthesis (na1, na2, brd1), Gα signaling (ct2), and three recently map-based/transgenically cloned architecture genes: ZmRPH1 (QWRF microtubule-associated protein), ZmDLE1 (EMS dwarf, distal chr1 Bin1.09; mutant cuts ear height ~63% vs PH ~35%), and ZmACS7 (ethylene/ACC-synthase, Sdw3; reduces PH and increases leaf angle — Li et al. 2020, Plant Physiol 183:1184). Teosinte is taller and more branched than B73, so teosinte ancestry is broadly height-increasing and partly collinear with K; as in DTA/PH, the mixed model absorbs ancestry-correlated variance, and the model-independent JLM (nested-in-family stepwise) is the primary QTL tool.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/eh_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/eh_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/eh_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/eh_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "EH"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ZEAL/BZea Ear-Height (EH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize plant-height candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). Candidates are a curated panel of cloned maize plant-architecture/height genes (auxin, GA, BR, Gα, plus the recently cloned ZmRPH1/ZmDLE1/ZmACS7; v5 coordinates); pathway records the hormone/growth pathway and protein each acts in.


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/eh_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("EH(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("EH candidates (B73 v5; curated maize plant-architecture/height panel): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)

title: ZEAL/BZea Ear-Height (EH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: OLS → MLM: structure control on a polygenic trait
text:
OLS → MLM: structure control on a polygenic trait
The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD, unfixable by any fixed factor); K deflates λ toward 1 and removes the ancestry-correlated bulk that a near-isogenic, teosinte-ancestry-structured height panel carries.


Show code
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea EH — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_ols,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/eh_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)









The OLS Manhattan (no K) is genome-wide inflated (ancestry-block LD) — the same structure artifact the MLM K term removes below.


Show code
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n > perm 5%` = c(nsig(ols$P, thr_ols), nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan's 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Ear-Height (EH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)
EH QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, entry by a 1000-permutation empirical α = 0.05 (Chen-style genome-wide FWER; per-trait)). JLM QTL are selected by the stepwise enter/exit P (a model-selection gate), independent of the per-scan permutation FWER line used for the GWAS Manhattans below. Model-independent — the primary EH analysis, as in Chen 2019.


Show code
p_lolli <- plot_lollipop(
  here("results/sim/zeal/tassel/eh_jlm_native1.txt"), mlm,
  "ZEAL/BZea EH — JLM QTL genomic distribution (nested in taxon)",
  overlap_csv = overlap,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/eh_jlm_lollipop_snp50k.png")
)
p_lolli

title: ZEAL/BZea Ear-Height (EH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated — the 70%-missing per-SNP panel at 0.4× is sparse — but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing, and the demonstration that ancestry inference is mandatory at low coverage — identical in mechanism to the DTA/PH notebooks.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("EH — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("EH — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/eh_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

title: ZEAL/BZea Ear-Height (EH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Multi-caller EH scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller EH scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same EH MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth (not a coverage sweep). The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below). Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (EH) changes.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/eh_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
# best (max -log10P) EH candidate window per caller — EH is polygenic, no single headline gene
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/eh_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "EH MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the EH plant-architecture candidate windows.")

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. The ZEAL/BZea analog of teonam-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-118k, built from real BZea data end-to-end: field DTA → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is 1,403 near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa (Zx mexicana 573, Zv parviglumis 383, Zd diploperennis 254, Zl luxurians 118, Zh huehuetenangensis 61) — the analog of TeoNAM’s 5 TIL families. The DTA BLUE is the per-field SpATS mean across two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5; cross-year genetic corr r = 0.675).
The central result mirrors TeoNAM: K = teosinte-ancestry relatedness is collinear with the flowering QTL, so the mixed model absorbs the very signal it protects — the flowering candidates (ZmCCT10, ZCN8) are suppressed under MLM. As in Chen 2019, the DTA QTL are therefore the province of JLM (nested-in-family stepwise), not the MLM Manhattan.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/dta_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/dta_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/dta_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/dta_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv. thr() reads the
# -log10P line for a panel; a marker is significant iff its -log10P exceeds that line.
TR <- "DTA"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic") # OLS runs on the RTIGER mosaic
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic") # Panel C
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt") # Panel B (per-SNP)
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE) # markers above the FWER line

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize flowering candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). The same v5 flowering candidates as TeoNAM (these are maize genes; teosinte introgression at them drives DTA in both populations).


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/dta_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("DTA(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], QTL = gg$qtl[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("DTA candidates (B73 v5): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: OLS → MLM: the flowering confound and structure control
text:
OLS → MLM: the flowering confound and structure control
The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD, unfixable by any fixed factor); K deflates λ toward 1 and collapses the flowering peaks (ZmCCT10), the ancestry-QTL confound.


Show code
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea DTA — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_ols,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/dta_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)









The OLS Manhattan (no K) is genome-wide inflated (λ_GC ≈ 2.4, ancestry-block LD) — many markers cross even the permutation line, and ZmCCT10 is nominally strong — precisely the structure artifact the MLM K term removes below.


Show code

  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),

  `n > perm 5%` = c(nsig(ols$P, thr_ols), nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)
DTA QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, entry by a 1000-permutation empirical α = 0.05 (Chen-style genome-wide FWER; per-trait)). JLM QTL are selected by the stepwise enter/exit P (a model-selection gate), independent of the per-scan permutation FWER line used for the GWAS Manhattans below. Model-independent — Chen’s primary DTA analysis.

)
