title: TeoNAM STAM — GWAS Manhattan with candidate genes (Chen 2019 Fig 4C)
section: RTIGER ancestry vs sequencing coverage (degradation sweep)
text:
RTIGER ancestry vs sequencing coverage (degradation sweep)
The panel-C baseline above is the λ = ∞, perfect-caller curve (GWAS on the interpolated truth genotypes). All sweeps below run on the same full 51,004-marker GWAS grid as the Fig-4C panels above. Here we ask how low-coverage sequencing degrades that signal through the RTIGER ancestry caller: for each family × coverage λ ∈ {0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 20}× we simulate reads from the real genotypes (.draw_counts, pi_floor=0, k_decay=1, error=0.01), call ancestry with call_ancestry(caller = "rtiger", rigidity = 2) (the calibrated rigidity_star), step-interpolate the recovered block onto the same union grid, and rerun the same STAM ~ Family + marker scan. Compute lives in scripts/teonam_rtiger_sweep.R --generate; this chunk only loads the combined sweep CSV and plots.
The read as a line-Manhattan (geom_line, one line per coverage; chromosomes never connect because group = interaction(CHR, coverage)): coverage on a viridis ramp, the λ = ∞ truth baseline in black as the reference curve, LOD = 5 dotted. The striking result is how robust RTIGER is — ancestry is a long-block signal, so even 0.1× (~9.5 % of markers carrying a read) reconstructs the blocks well enough that the tb1 peak only slips from ≈17.3 (baseline) to ≈16.1. Degradation is real but lives mostly in HET recovery (het-recall 0.77 at 0.1× → 1.00 at ≥20×), not in the additive QTL peaks.


Show code
p_sweep <- plot_sweep_line(
  here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_rtiger_sweep.csv"),
  "STAM — RTIGER ancestry, GWAS −log10P vs coverage",
  base_size = 15
)
ggsave(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_rtiger_sweep_manhattan.png"),
  p_sweep,
  width = 9, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white"
)
p_sweep

title: TeoNAM STAM — GWAS Manhattan with candidate genes (Chen 2019 Fig 4C)
section: Coverage-degradation composite: interpolation control / nNIL / RTIGER / LB-Impute
text:
Coverage-degradation composite: interpolation control / nNIL / RTIGER / LB-Impute
The four-panel contrast (agent/teonam-control-sweep-plan.md). All four degrade the identical truth mosaics over the same coverage grid λ ∈ {0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 20}× (+ the λ = ∞ interpolated-truth baseline) and feed the same STAM ~ Family + marker GWAS — only the genotype caller differs:

A — interpolation control (nilHMM::call_gt(prior = "hwe") + per-RIL step-interpolation; scripts/teonam_control_sweep.R): the deliberately het-prone no-HMM caller — a single ALT read is called HET via the 2p(1−p) HWE prior term.
B — nNIL (call_ancestry(caller = "nnil", rrate = rrate_star, err = 0.01), f_1 = 0.08, f_2 = 0.15; scripts/teonam_nnil_sweep.R): count emission + geometric duration.
C — RTIGER (caller = "rtiger", rigidity = 2; scripts/teonam_rtiger_sweep.R): count emission + rigidity duration.
D — LB-Impute (call_ancestry(caller = "lbimpute", unit = "cm", recombdist = recombdist_star ≈ 5.5); scripts/teonam_lbimpute_sweep.R): coverage-aware emission + distance-dependent (map-aware cM) transition with a double-recombination penalty, full-chromosome Viterbi (nilHMM native port of Fragoso et al. 2014). recombdist is the donor-fragment-Dice-optimal recombdist_star from the BC1S4 calibration (scripts/02_calibrate.R), read from calib_params.csv — not hardcoded.

The contrast is het handling. The three duration/distance-aware HMMs (B, C, D) tie neighbouring markers into ancestry blocks, so a lone ALT read cannot open a het call — het-call fraction stays near the ~8 % truth level and the STAM peaks hold near the baseline down to 0.1×. LB-Impute (D), at the calibrated recombdist_star ≈ 5.5 cM, degrades gracefully like B and C rather than over-smoothing: its tb1 peak holds near the baseline across the grid (≈16.3 at 0.1× vs ≈17.3 baseline), never over-shooting. (The tighter calibrated distance scale allows more transitions than the earlier hardcoded recombdist = 50, which had produced over-long imputed homozygous blocks and an over-shot peak.) The control (A) decides each site independently, so at low coverage the HWE prior inflates het to ~20 % (het excess) and the tb1 peak degrades the other way (≈13.4 at 0.1× vs ≈17.3 baseline). geom_line, colored by coverage, no gene labels, LOD = 5, chromosomes never connect (group = interaction(CHR, coverage)).
A pericentromeric block-imputation “ghost” on chromosome 3 at 0.1×. At the lowest coverage a spurious peak appears near the middle of chr3 (≈110–115 Mb, on the chromosome axis label) — proximal to both chr3 STAM candidates, tru1 (STAM3.1, ≈152 Mb) and zmm16 (STAM3.2, ≈176 Mb), which sit in the right half of the chromosome. It is a false positive: the λ = ∞ baseline has no peak there (≈8.5), whereas the 0.1× line rises to ≈12.4 (excess ≈+3.9 over baseline). The ghost sits on the proximal shoulder of the chr3 pericentromere — the restricted-recombination zone reported at ≈80–90 Mb in B73 v5 (pan-genome cenhap boundaries) and independently visible in this study’s own native cM map, where the recombination rate collapses to ≈0.06 cM/Mb across ≈80–100 Mb (~40× below the ≈2 cM/Mb near the telomeres). Recombination is so suppressed there that cM barely advances across tens of Mb, so with the map-aware cM transition adjacent markers are separated by ~zero cM and the state stays highly persistent; at 0.1× (only ~9.5 % of markers carrying a read) LB-Impute lays down a long spurious homozygous block through this low-recombination valley that tracks the phenotype. It is a block-caller effect: the no-HMM interpolation control (A) shows no central ghost (excess ≈0), nNIL (B) and RTIGER (C) show milder versions (excess ≈+2.3–2.6), and LB-Impute (D) is the most affected (≈+3.9).
Note this ghost is a genuine consequence of the calibrated recombdist_star (≈5.5 cM): with the earlier hardcoded recombdist = 50 the chr3 middle stayed near baseline (excess ≈+1) and the over-imputation instead landed on the right, inflating the real zmm16 region (≈16.7 vs ≈14.8 baseline). The tighter calibrated distance scale represents the real peaks more faithfully but, interacting with pericentromeric cM compression that the BC1S4 calibration sim does not model, produces this central false block at extreme low coverage. Treat the 0.1× chr3-mid peak as a coverage-limit artifact, not signal.


Show code
het_ctrl <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_control_het_fraction.csv"))[, .(coverage, control = round(het_frac, 3))]
het_nnil <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_nnil_het_fraction.csv"))[, .(coverage, nnil = round(het_frac, 3))]
het_tab <- merge(het_ctrl, het_nnil, by = "coverage")
het_tab[] # HET-call fraction vs coverage: control (het excess at low λ) vs nNIL (flat ~truth)

  "STAM — interpolation control (GL + HWE)",
  legend = TRUE

pB <- plot_sweep_line(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_nnil_sweep.csv"),
  "STAM — nNIL ancestry",
  legend = FALSE

pC <- plot_sweep_line(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_rtiger_sweep.csv"),

  ncol = 1, align = "v", axis = "lr",

title: TeoNAM STAM — GWAS Manhattan with candidate genes (Chen 2019 Fig 4C)
section: Is the chr3-mid ghost a real QTL? Structure/kinship control at 0.1× (MLM Q + K, LOCO)
text:
Is the chr3-mid ghost a real QTL? Structure/kinship control at 0.1× (MLM Q + K, LOCO)
The composite above is an OLS scan (STAM ~ Family + marker), which models neither kinship nor fine population structure — exactly what inflates broad signals in low-recombination regions. Chen 2019’s GWAS instead used a mixed model with Q = 5 PCs + K = kinship. To test whether the 0.1× chr3-pericentromere ghost is a real QTL or a structure/LD confound, we re-scan the same 0.1× LB-Impute genotypes three ways (compute in scripts/teonam_mlm_loco_lb01.R — an EMMAX port: VanRaden K + 5 PCs + REML δ + P3D GLS; genotype matrix from scripts/teonam_lbimpute_sweep.R --generate --save-matrix):

A — OLS (Family): the composite’s model. Ghost present (chr3-mid ≈12.4).
B — MLM (Q + K): kinship + structure controlled (λ_GC = 1.10). The ghost collapses to ≈4.7, back below the LOD-5 line — the signature of a structure/LD confound, not a QTL. (The real tru1/zmm16 peaks are pulled down here too — the known MLM proximal over-correction on their own chromosome.)
C — LOCO: kinship built excluding the tested chromosome. The real QTL return (tru1/159/zmm16 ≈ 9.6–12.6) — but so does the ghost (≈10.7), because dropping chr3 from the kinship removes the correction for its own pericentromeric structure. At 0.1× the ghost becomes indistinguishable from a real QTL under LOCO.

Red dashed line = the chr3 pericentromere ghost (~112 Mb); the tru1/zmm16 stars mark the real distal QTL. Takeaway: the ghost is a structure/LD confound that full-K MLM controls even at 0.1×, but low-coverage block imputation inflates it enough to nearly survive Q + K and to fully resurface under LOCO — so LOCO peaks in low-recombination regions cannot be trusted at low coverage.


Show code
lb01 <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_lbimpute_lambda0.1.csv")) # OLS, Family only
ml <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/mlm_loco_scan_lb01.csv")) # MLM Q+K (p_full) + LOCO (p_loco)

ols_s <- lb01[is.finite(P) & P > 0, .(SNP, CHR, BP, P)]
full_s <- ml[is.finite(p_full) & p_full > 0, .(SNP = marker, CHR = chr, BP = bp, P = p_full)]
loco_s <- ml[is.finite(p_loco) & p_loco > 0, .(SNP = marker, CHR = chr, BP = bp, P = p_loco)]

ghost <- list(chr = 3, bp = 112e6) # chr3 pericentromere ghost locus
pO <- plot_stam_manhattan(ols_s, "STAM — LB-Impute 0.1×, OLS (Family)", mark = ghost)
pF <- plot_stam_manhattan(full_s, "STAM — LB-Impute 0.1×, MLM (Q + K)", mark = ghost)
pL <- plot_stam_manhattan(loco_s, "STAM — LB-Impute 0.1×, LOCO", mark = ghost)

ghost_grid <- cowplot::plot_grid(pO, pF, pL,
  ncol = 1, align = "v", axis = "lr",
  labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 18
)
ggsave(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_ghost_mlm_loco.png"), ghost_grid,
  width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white"
)
ghost_grid











Show code
regmax <- function(dt, pcol, lo, hi) {
  z <- dt[chr == 3 & bp >= lo * 1e6 & bp <= hi * 1e6]
  pv <- z[[pcol]][is.finite(z[[pcol]]) & z[[pcol]] > 0]
  if (length(pv)) round(max(-log10(pv)), 1) else NA_real_
}
olsdt <- copy(lb01)
setnames(olsdt, c("CHR", "BP", "P"), c("chr", "bp", "p_ols"))
gtab <- data.table(
  region = c("pericentromere 80–120 (ghost)", "tru1 ~152", "159-mid", "zmm16 ~176"),
  lo = c(80, 150, 157, 174), hi = c(120, 154, 162, 178)
)
gtab[, `:=`(
  OLS = mapply(function(a, b) regmax(olsdt, "p_ols", a, b), lo, hi),
  `MLM (Q+K)` = mapply(function(a, b) regmax(ml, "p_full", a, b), lo, hi),
  LOCO = mapply(function(a, b) regmax(ml, "p_loco", a, b), lo, hi)
)]
gtab[, .(region, OLS, `MLM (Q+K)`, LOCO)] # chr3 max -log10P at 0.1x; LOD = 5


                          region   OLS MLM (Q+K)  LOCO
                          <char> <num>     <num> <num>
1: pericentromere 80–120 (ghost)  12.4       4.7  10.7
2:                     tru1 ~152  13.7       4.8  12.6
3:                       159-mid  12.3       4.2  11.3
4:                    zmm16 ~176  13.7       2.4   9.6

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
What this is. The GWAS model Chen 2019 actually used for Fig 4C — a linear mixed model with population structure (Q) and kinship (K) — run in TASSEL5 (native arm64), and contrasted with the fixed-effect OLS scan (teonam-stam-ols-gwas-family-imputed). A mixed model needs a complete genotype matrix, so this runs on the step-interpolated complete matrix (teonam_map_v5_gwas, 51,004 markers × 1,237 lines), not the sparse observed set. This is the full GWAS marker set — the same 51,004 markers as the family-imputed OLS scan (cross-family gaps densified rather than left NA) — so the OLS↔︎MLM comparison is like-for-like on one marker set. The ~3,300 markers that share a consensus-map cM interpolate to identical genotype rows (and identical p-values); they keep their real bp, so they appear as short terraces on the Manhattan rather than being dropped. Compute lives in scripts/teonam_mlm_interp.R; this notebook loads, documents, and plots.
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
theme_set(theme_bw(base_size = 13))
LOD <- 5   # Chen 2019 significance line: P < 1e-5

# genomic inflation factor: median(chi^2_obs) / median(chi^2_null)
lambda_gc <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, df = 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, df = 1), 3)
}

# Manhattan in the teonam-stam-ols-gwas-family-imputed style (odd chr grey60 / even black,
# LOD dotted, chromosome axis). Input: data.table(CHR, BP, logP).
manhattan <- function(d, title) {
  d <- d[order(CHR, BP)]
  off <- d[, .(mx = max(BP)), by = CHR][order(CHR)]
  off[, base := cumsum(as.numeric(shift(mx, fill = 0)))]
  d <- merge(d, off[, .(CHR, base)], by = "CHR")
  d[, x := as.numeric(BP) + base]
  axis <- d[, .(mid = (min(x) + max(x)) / 2), by = CHR][order(CHR)]
  ggplot(d, aes(x, logP, colour = factor(CHR %% 2))) +
    geom_point(size = 0.5) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = LOD, linetype = "dotted") +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c("grey60", "black"), guide = "none") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = axis$mid, labels = axis$CHR, expand = c(0.01, 0)) +
    labs(x = "chromosome", y = expression(-log[10](italic(P))), title = title)
}

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
The model
The previous notebook fit OLS (fixed effects only):
\[
y_i \;=\; \mu \;+\; \sum_f \beta_f\,\mathbb{1}[\text{family}_i=f] \;+\; \gamma\,g_{im} \;+\; \varepsilon_i .
\]
Chen’s GWAS fits a linear mixed model (Yu et al. 2006), adding a polygenic random term with covariance given by the kinship matrix:
\[
\mathbf{y} \;=\; \mathbf{1}\mu \;+\; \mathbf{Q}\boldsymbol{\alpha}
\;+\; \mathbf{g}_a\,\gamma_a \;+\; \mathbf{g}_d\,\gamma_d
\;+\; \mathbf{Z}\mathbf{u} \;+\; \boldsymbol{\varepsilon},
\qquad
\mathbf{u}\sim N(\mathbf 0,\,\mathbf K\sigma^2_g),\;\;
\boldsymbol{\varepsilon}\sim N(\mathbf 0,\,\mathbf I\sigma^2_e).
\]

\(\mathbf{Q}\) — first 5 principal components (fixed, population structure).
\(\mathbf{g}_a\) — additive marker dosage (0/1/2), effect \(\gamma_a\); \(\mathbf{g}_d\) — dominance indicator (1 if heterozygous), effect \(\gamma_d\). Both are TASSEL’s default; the marker is tested by the joint 2-df hypothesis \(H_0:\gamma_a=\gamma_d=0\).
\(\mathbf{u}\) — polygenic background; \(\mathbf{K}\) = centered IBS kinship.

Estimation (TASSEL, P3D). Variance components \((\sigma^2_g,\sigma^2_e)\) are fit by REML via the EMMA spectral trick (eigendecompose \(\mathbf K\) once), estimated once under the null and reused for every marker (P3D). Each marker is then a generalized-least-squares \(F\)-test (2 df: \(\gamma_a,\gamma_d\)) using \(\mathbf V^{-1}\), \(\mathbf V=\mathbf K\sigma^2_g+\mathbf I\sigma^2_e\). (The additive-only 1-df test, TASSEL’s add_p, is the alternative — arguably better for a ~homozygous RIL panel; it gives \(\lambda_{GC}\approx0.97\) vs 1.37 for the joint test.)
Why it matters. OLS omits \(\mathbf{Z}\mathbf{u}\) — no polygenic shrinkage — so test statistics inflate under structure/relatedness. The MLM’s \(\mathbf K\) term is exactly the correction; the effect is quantified below as the drop in \(\lambda_{GC}\).

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
Pipeline (scripts/teonam_mlm_interp.R, native arm64 TASSEL5)

# 1. densify the full 51,004 GWAS set per chr by step-interpolation (nilHMM) -> complete
#    51,004 x 1,237 matrix (no missing) -> hapmap geno_gwas_interp.hmp.txt
# 2. K: TASSEL Centered_IBS on the complete matrix
run_pipeline.pl -importGuess geno_gwas_interp.hmp.txt \
  -KinshipPlugin -method Centered_IBS -endPlugin -export kinship_cIBS_interp.txt -exportType SqrMatrix
# 3. Q: 5 PCs via prcomp(complete matrix) -> phenotype covariates (STAM + PC1..5)
# 4. MLM (P3D): genotype + phenotype(+Q) + K, two-stage combine
run_pipeline.pl -fork1 -h geno_gwas_interp.hmp.txt -fork2 -r pheno_stam_pc_interp.txt \
  -fork3 -k kinship_cIBS_interp.txt -combine4 -input1 -input2 -intersect \
  -combine5 -input4 -input3 -mlm -mlmVarCompEst P3D -mlmCompressionLevel None -export mlm_interp


tim <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/mlm_interp_timings.csv"))
knitr::kable(tim, caption = "TASSEL5 step timings (native arm64, Temurin 17; MLM uses pure-Java EJML, no native BLAS)")

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
Load results

ols <- fread(here("data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan_interpolated.csv"))            # OLS on the interpolated 51,004
ols <- ols[is.finite(P) & P > 0, .(CHR = as.integer(CHR), BP = as.integer(BP), P)]
mlm <- fread(here("data/teonam/tassel/mlm_interp2.txt"))        # TASSEL MLM (for the gene table)
mlm <- mlm[Marker != "None", .(CHR = as.integer(Chr), BP = as.integer(Pos), P = as.numeric(p))][is.finite(P) & P > 0]
lc  <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/mlm_loco_scan.csv"))      # p_full = MLM Q+K (faithful, = TASSEL), p_loco = LOCO

# the three models on the same interpolated 51,004-marker matrix
three <- rbindlist(list(
  ols[, .(CHR, BP, P, method = "OLS (no K)")],
  lc[is.finite(p_full) & p_full > 0, .(CHR = chr, BP = bp, P = p_full, method = "MLM Q+K")],
  lc[is.finite(p_loco) & p_loco > 0, .(CHR = chr, BP = bp, P = p_loco, method = "LOCO")]
))
lev  <- c("OLS (no K)", "MLM Q+K", "LOCO")
labs <- setNames(vapply(lev, function(m) sprintf("%s, λ=%.2f", m, lambda_gc(three[method == m, P])), ""), lev)
three[, mlab := factor(labs[method], levels = labs[lev])]
pal  <- setNames(c("firebrick", "steelblue4", "darkorange"), labs[lev])

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
Genomic inflation: OLS → MLM → LOCO

knitr::kable(
  three[, .(markers = .N, lambda_GC = lambda_gc(P), max_logP = round(max(-log10(P)), 2)), by = method],
  caption = "Three models on the same 51,004 interpolated markers (only the structure correction differs)."
)

The Q + K correction pulls \(\lambda_{GC}\) from 2.55 (OLS) → 1.37 (MLM) and the global peak from 17.3 → 8.7. LOCO (leave-one-chromosome-out kinship — the diagnostic detailed below) trades global control for local power, so its \(\lambda_{GC}\) runs high (≈3.4) while it revives the true peaks. Residual \(\lambda>1\) under the MLM is interpolation ties (pseudo-replication) plus structure not fully captured by 5 PCs.

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
QQ — OLS · MLM (Q+K) · LOCO (one panel)

qd <- three[, {
  p <- P[is.finite(P) & P > 0 & P <= 1]; o <- -log10(sort(p)); e <- -log10(ppoints(length(p)))
  k <- c(which(o > 2), sample(which(o <= 2), min(sum(o <= 2), 6000)))
  .(exp = e[k], obs = o[k])
}, by = mlab]
ggplot(qd, aes(exp, obs, colour = mlab)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, colour = "grey40", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2.5, alpha = 1))) +
  labs(x = expression(Expected~-log[10](italic(P))), y = expression(Observed~-log[10](italic(P))),
       title = "STAM GWAS QQ: OLS vs MLM (Q+K) vs LOCO") +
  theme(legend.position = c(0.02, 0.98), legend.justification = c(0, 1),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = scales::alpha("white", 0.7), colour = NA))








MLM (blue) hugs the null through the bulk; OLS (red) is inflated everywhere; LOCO (orange) restores the true tail but at a globally inflated baseline.

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
section: Candidate-gene peaks: what the correction removes
text:
Candidate-gene peaks: what the correction removes
The MLM deflates the whole genome; several JLM candidate genes that clear LOD 5 under OLS drop below threshold under the mixed model — including tb1, tsh4, and zmm16. The LOCO test below confirms this is over-correction (proximal contamination — the peaks return when kinship excludes their own chromosome), not evidence against the genes.

ov <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_candidate_overlap.csv"))   # symbol, chr, start (v5)
peakfn <- function(scan, ch, ps) {
  w <- scan[CHR == ch & abs(BP - ps) <= 5e5]
  if (nrow(w)) round(max(-log10(w$P)), 2) else NA_real_
}
gt <- data.table(
  gene = ov$symbol, chr = ov$chr,
  OLS = mapply(function(c, p) peakfn(ols, c, p), ov$chr, ov$start),
  MLM = mapply(function(c, p) peakfn(mlm, c, p), ov$chr, ov$start)
)
gt[, `OLS>5` := ifelse(is.finite(OLS) & OLS >= LOD, "yes", "no")]
gt[, `MLM>5` := ifelse(is.finite(MLM) & MLM >= LOD, "yes", "no")]
knitr::kable(gt[order(-OLS)], caption = "Candidate-gene peak -log10P (±500 kb): OLS vs MLM, and LOD-5 survival.")

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
section: LOCO test — is the loss over-correction?
text:
LOCO test — is the loss over-correction?
Is the MLM’s loss of tb1/tsh4/zmm16 genuine deflation or proximal contamination — the kinship absorbing each QTL’s own-chromosome signal? The standard diagnostic is LOCO: build the kinship from all markers except the tested marker’s chromosome. Implemented as an EMMAX in R (scripts/teonam_mlm_loco.R) that uses TASSEL’s model exactly — additive + dominance, 2-df joint F, P3D — and reproduces the full-K TASSEL MLM (λ ≈ 1.366 vs 1.365; per-gene peaks within ~0.1): a full-K pass (validation) and the LOCO pass.

lc <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/mlm_loco_scan.csv"))     # marker, chr, bp, p_full, p_loco
peak2 <- function(pv, ch, ps) {
  w <- lc[chr == ch & abs(bp - ps) <= 5e5]; v <- -log10(w[[pv]]); v <- v[is.finite(v)]
  if (length(v)) round(max(v), 2) else NA_real_
}
loco <- data.table(
  gene = ov$symbol, chr = ov$chr,
  OLS  = gt$OLS[match(ov$symbol, gt$gene)],
  MLM  = gt$MLM[match(ov$symbol, gt$gene)],
  `EMMAX full-K` = mapply(function(c, s) peak2("p_full", c, s), ov$chr, ov$start),
  LOCO = mapply(function(c, s) peak2("p_loco", c, s), ov$chr, ov$start)
)
knitr::kable(loco[order(-OLS)], caption = sprintf(
  "Candidate-gene peak -log10P — LOCO restores the peaks (λ_GC: full-K %.2f, LOCO %.2f).",
  lambda_gc(lc$p_full), lambda_gc(lc$p_loco)))

(The full-K-vs-LOCO contrast is the middle vs bottom facet of the Manhattan above.)
Reading. The peaks return under LOCO (tb1 4.2→11.0, zmm16 4.0→10.0, tru1 5.7→12.5, an1 4.4→7.1, tsh4 2.7→5.0) — only possible if the full-K MLM was absorbing each QTL’s own-chromosome signal. So the loss is over-correction (proximal contamination), not evidence against the genes. (ts2 stays low even under LOCO — its OLS signal was genuinely mostly confounding, echoing the original ~9-vs-15 concern.)
Two notes. (1) This R-EMMAX is faithful, not an approximation: it uses TASSEL’s exact model (add+dom, 2-df joint F, P3D) and its full-K pass reproduces the TASSEL MLM (λ_GC = 1.372 vs 1.365; peaks within ~0.1). Kinship flavor is irrelevant — VanRaden and TASSEL’s Centered-IBS gave identical results. It’s also ~15× faster (R’s native Accelerate BLAS ≈30 s vs TASSEL’s pure-Java EJML ≈7 min), so it’s the engine of choice for the coverage sweep. (2) LOCO’s own genome-wide λ_GC (3.426) is inflated — it trades global control for local power on this heavily-structured interpolated panel, so LOCO here is a diagnostic, not a fix to deploy. The real fix remains chen2019_GWAS (118K directly-typed).

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
Notes

Canonical, tracked source for the MLM scan: scripts/teonam_mlm_interp.R (build → K → Q → MLM) + scripts/teonam_mlm_lambda.R (λ_GC / QQ). Outputs: data/teonam/tassel/mlm_interp2.txt, results/sim/teonam/mlm_interp_timings.csv.
Matrix: interpolated-complete (teonam_map_v5_gwas, 51,004) — required because the MLM needs a complete matrix (the sparse observed set stalls: per-marker missing defeats P3D reuse). Cost: block plateaus, no scatter.
Faithful GWAS (scatter + correct absolute peaks) needs chen2019_GWAS (118,838 directly-typed SNPs); see DATA.md and the outreach in agent/outreach_teonam_118k.md.

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
Model-specification caveats & suggestions
This MLM reproduces TASSEL faithfully, but it is not the best-specified model for this population — the QQ shows no correction reaches \(\lambda_{GC}\approx1\). Four issues, roughly in order of importance, and how to address each:

Common marker effect in a NAM (most important). The five teosinte founders (TIL01/03/11/14/25) carry different alleles, so a single additive slope on the 0/1/2 dosage pools them as if identical. The correctly-specified model nests the marker within family (family + family:marker) — which is exactly why Chen’s primary method was the JLM, with the common-effect GWAS only a complementary approximation. Fix: use the nested model; don’t treat the common-effect MLM as the target.
Additive + dominance on a ~homozygous RIL panel. The dominance term is here only to match TASSEL’s default — but with ~8% heterozygosity it is barely estimable and mostly injects noise (it is what pushes \(\lambda_{GC}\) from 0.97 to 1.37). Additive-only (1 df) is the defensible spec for RILs.
Structure term. We use \(\mathbf{Q}\) = 5 PCs (Chen’s GWAS choice); the natural cofactor for a five-family NAM is the Family factor (4 df). Note the inconsistency: the OLS scan used Family, this MLM uses PCs.
Infinitesimal-\(\mathbf{K}\) vs the actual architecture. The MLM assumes many small polygenic effects; STAM is a few large domestication QTL, and the interpolated genotypes are block-correlated (pseudo-replication). Those violations are why \(\lambda_{GC}\) stays off 1 regardless of the correction — no kinship trick fixes a fundamentally mis-modeled null.

title: TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the family-imputed map panel (Fig 4C)
text:
What this is. The computation behind the STAM OLS GWAS scan on the family-imputed map panel (data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan_family_imputed.csv), tracked in its own right. It ports the throwaway scripts/teonam_stam_gwas_family_imputed.R into a rendered notebook so the scan — not just its Manhattan — is reproducible from primary data. The annotated Fig-4C Manhattan that consumes this CSV lives in teonam-qtl-recovery; this notebook produces it.
The genotypes are FSFHap family-imputed (teonam_map_v5_gwas, 51,004 markers — Chen’s map construction, complete within family). Each marker is tested only on the families that typed it (cross-family gaps left as NA, not densified), giving the natural GWAS scatter with no step-interpolation plateau artifact. (family_imputed names exactly this state: FSFHap within-family imputation only, without cross-family step-interpolation — contrast the interpolated matrix used by the MLM notebook.)
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(parallel)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
theme_set(theme_bw(base_size = 12))
THREADS <- max(1L, detectCores() - 2L)
LOD <- 5 # Chen 2019 significance line: P < 1e-5

title: TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the family-imputed map panel (Fig 4C)
text:
Inputs (all primary data — no .rds intermediates)

# FSFHap-imputed recoded dosages (complete within family): 1237 lines x 51,482 markers; NA = cross-family untyped (0/1/2 = W22 / het / teosinte)
geno <- fread(here("data/teonam/TeoNAM_genotype_clean.csv"))

# robust key = family + line, e.g. "TIL01" + "A001"; family from the population column
key <- paste0(sub("^W22", "", geno[[2]]), sub("^.*Line_", "", geno[[1]]))
fam <- factor(sub("^W22", "", geno[[2]]))

# phenotype: STAM (staminate spikelet), joined by line key
ph <- as.data.frame(read_excel(
  here("data/teonam/9250682/TeoNAM_1257RILs_22traits_phenotype_data.xlsx")
))
names(ph)[1] <- "line"
y <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(setNames(ph$STAM, ph$line)[key]))

# v5 coordinates; restrict the scan to markers that lifted to v5
mc <- fread(here("data/teonam/markers_v5.tsv"))
chr_by <- setNames(mc$chr_v5, mc$marker)
pos_by <- setNames(mc$pos_v5, mc$marker)
mk <- intersect(names(geno)[-(1:3)], mc$marker)

data.table(
  quantity = c(
    "lines", "markers (raw)", "markers (lifted, scanned)",
    "STAM phenotyped", "families"
  ),
  value = c(
    nrow(geno), ncol(geno) - 3L, length(mk),
    sum(!is.na(y)), nlevels(fam)
  )
)

                      <char> <int>
1:                     lines  1237
2:             markers (raw) 51482
3: markers (lifted, scanned) 51004
4:           STAM phenotyped  1236
5:                  families     5

title: TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the family-imputed map panel (Fig 4C)
text:
The model
For each marker \(m\), restrict to the lines \(i\) with a non-missing dosage \(g_{im}\in\{0,1,2\}\) and a non-missing STAM value \(y_i\), then compare two nested least-squares fits:
\[
\textbf{Null:}\quad y_i = \mu + \sum_{f} \beta_f \,\mathbb{1}[\text{family}_i = f] + \varepsilon_i
\] \[
\textbf{Full:}\quad y_i = \mu + \sum_{f} \beta_f \,\mathbb{1}[\text{family}_i = f] + \gamma\, g_{im} + \varepsilon_i
\]
The marker enters as a single additive dosage effect \(\gamma\) shared across families (1 numerator df). Significance is the marginal \(F\)-test of \(\gamma\):
\[
F = \frac{(\mathrm{RSS}_0 - \mathrm{RSS}_1)/1}{\mathrm{RSS}_1/(n - \mathrm{rank})},
\qquad P = \Pr\big(F_{1,\,n-\mathrm{rank}} > F\big).
\]
Family enters as a fixed categorical covariate (levels dropped to those present for that marker) — it plays the role of the structure term. This is ordinary least squares (OLS): there is no kinship/polygenic random effect \(K\). Chen 2019 instead used a mixed model y ~ Q(5 PCs) + SNP + K(IBS); the missing random \(K\) is what makes ours “not mixed”, so peak heights here are expected to run hotter — quantifying that inflation (\(\lambda_{GC}\), QQ) is a separate analysis.

  geom_point(size = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = LOD, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("grey60", "black"), guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = axis$mid, labels = axis$CHR, expand = c(0.01, 0)) +
  labs(
    x = "chromosome", y = expression(-log[10](italic(P))),
    title = "STAM family-imputed GWAS (OLS: STAM ~ Family + marker)"
  )

title: TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the family-imputed map panel (Fig 4C)
text:
Notes

This notebook is the canonical, tracked source for stam_gwas_scan_family_imputed.csv; it supersedes the untracked scripts/teonam_stam_gwas_family_imputed.R (identical model, ported verbatim).
Model is OLS (a fixed-effects linear model) with no kinship term — deliberately faithful to the throwaway script, not to Chen’s MLM. The peak-height / \(\lambda_{GC}\) comparison against Chen’s Q + K mixed model is deferred to a dedicated inflation notebook.

title: Inv4m tagging-SNP genotype: 50K vs 200K RTIGER calls, ZEAL NIL panel
text:
What this is. A companion to the inversion-paper ZEAL flowering notebook (scripts/inversion_paper/Zeal_Inv4m_flowering_lmm.Rmd). The genotype at the Inv4m tagging SNP PZE04175660223 (chr4:181,637,780) is read from two RTIGER ancestry call sets, both are RTIGER (HMM on skim allele counts); they differ only in the reference variant set:
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(scales)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(knitr)
source(here("R/plotting.R")) # state_palette(): REF=gold, HET=springgreen4, ALT=purple4

POS <- 181637780L # PZE04175660223, chr4
geno2 <- c(recurrent = "gold", donor = "purple4") # 2-state (presence)
geno3 <- state_palette() # REF / HET / ALT

title: Inv4m tagging-SNP genotype: 50K vs 200K RTIGER calls, ZEAL NIL panel
text:
Deriving the two genotype vectors


Show code
# --- 200K (previous): B73 / Introgression segment state at the locus, per line ---
L <- readRDS(here("data/zeal/rtiger_200K_calls_introfinder.rds")) # staged from BZea_Introgression_Finder
g250v <- sapply(L, function(tb) {
  tb <- as.data.frame(tb)
  s <- tb[tb$V1 == "chr4", , drop = FALSE]
  if (!nrow(s)) {
    return(NA_character_)
  }
  h <- s$V4[s$V2 <= POS & s$V3 >= POS]
  if (length(h)) h[1] else NA_character_
})
d250 <- data.table(
  NIL = sub("[.]B$", "", names(L)), # strip .B bulk suffix -> pedigree
  g250 = ifelse(is.na(g250v), NA_character_, ifelse(g250v == "B73", "recurrent", "donor"))
)
d250 <- unique(d250[!is.na(g250)], by = "NIL")

# --- 50K (current): 0/1/2 segment state at the locus, per line (via skim_id) ---
seg <- fread(here("data/zeal/rtiger_50K_calls.csv"))
ss <- fread(here("data/zeal/samplesheet_3way.csv"))
cov <- unique(seg[chr == 4L & start_bp <= POS & end_bp >= POS, .(skim_id = name, st = state)], by = "skim_id")
p2s <- unique(ss[!is.na(skim_id) & skim_id != "", .(NIL = pedigree, skim_id)], by = "NIL")
d50 <- merge(p2s, cov, by = "skim_id")[, .(NIL,
  g50_3 = factor(st, 0:2, c("REF", "HET", "ALT")),
  g50   = ifelse(st == 0L, "recurrent", "donor")
)]

# --- phenotypes + paper filters (CLY25, incl. paper's outlier plots) ---
OUTLIER_PLOTS <- c(2756, 453, 304)
cly <- fread(here("data/zeal/CLY25_ZEAL.csv"))
setnames(cly, "Genotype", "NIL")
meta <- fread(here("data/zeal/Bzea_metadata.csv"))
pheno <- cly[!Plot %in% OUTLIER_PLOTS & !NIL %in% c("B73", "Purple Check") & !is.na(NIL)]
pheno[, accession := substr(NIL, 1, 7)]
pheno[, ancestry := meta$race[match(accession, meta$donor_id)]]
pheno <- merge(pheno, d250[, .(NIL, g250)], by = "NIL", all.x = TRUE)
pheno <- merge(pheno, d50[, .(NIL, g50, g50_3)], by = "NIL", all.x = TRUE)

# per-line table, restricted to lines carrying BOTH calls (fair head-to-head)
byNIL <- unique(pheno[!is.na(g250) & !is.na(g50), .(NIL, g250, g50, g50_3)], by = "NIL")
cat(sprintf(
  "lines with 200K call: %d | 50K call: %d | BOTH: %d\n",
  uniqueN(d250$NIL), uniqueN(d50$NIL), nrow(byNIL)
))


lines with 200K call: 900 | 50K call: 1394 | BOTH: 886

title: Inv4m tagging-SNP genotype: 50K vs 200K RTIGER calls, ZEAL NIL panel
text:
1. Confusion matrix, 50K (current) vs 200K (previous)
The 200K set resolves only presence/absence (recurrent / donor), while the 50K set resolves all three genotypes. A cell is a match when the two sets agree on presence: REF ↔︎ recurrent, and HET or ALT ↔︎ donor. Match cells are filled with the 50K genotype colour (state_palette); mismatches are white.


Show code
cm <- CJ(
  g50_3 = factor(c("REF", "HET", "ALT"), c("REF", "HET", "ALT")),
  g250 = factor(c("recurrent", "donor"), c("recurrent", "donor"))
)
cnt <- byNIL[, .N, by = .(g50_3, g250)]
cm <- cnt[cm, on = .(g50_3, g250)][is.na(N), N := 0L]
cm[, match := (g50_3 == "REF" & g250 == "recurrent") | (g50_3 %in% c("HET", "ALT") & g250 == "donor")]
cm[, fillkey := ifelse(match, as.character(g50_3), "mismatch")]
cm[, g50_3 := factor(g50_3, levels = c("ALT", "HET", "REF"))] # ALT top → REF bottom

n_tot <- sum(cm$N)
n_match <- cm[match == TRUE, sum(N)]
fill_vals <- c(geno3, mismatch = "white")

p_cm <- ggplot(cm, aes(g250, g50_3, fill = fillkey)) +
  geom_tile(color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.8) +
  # match-cell counts in white; mismatch-cell counts in grey for legibility on white
  geom_text(data = cm[match == TRUE], aes(label = N), color = "white", size = 7, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(data = cm[match == FALSE], aes(label = N), color = "grey35", size = 6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_vals, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(
    position = "top", limits = c("recurrent", "donor"),
    labels = c(recurrent = "B73\n(recurrent)", donor = "Introgression\n(donor)")
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(REF = "REF\n(recurrent)", HET = "HET", ALT = "ALT\n(hom. donor)")) +
  labs(
    x = "200K (previous), Nirwan",
    y = "50K (current)\nFausto",
    title = "Inv4m RTIGER calls",
    subtitle = sprintf(
      "%d NILs with both lines,\n%.1f%% concordant on presence",
      n_tot, 100 * n_match / n_tot
    )
  ) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_classic2(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.line = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank()
  )
p_cm











Show code
dcast(cm, g50_3 ~ g250, value.var = "N")[order(-match(g50_3, c("REF", "HET", "ALT")))] |>
  kable(caption = "NIL counts, 50K genotype (rows) × 200K genotype (columns)")



NIL counts, 50K genotype (rows) × 200K genotype (columns)

donor

Reading it. The two RTIGER call sets agree on donor presence for 98.4% of lines. Where the 200K set calls Introgression, the 50K set resolves the zygosity into heterozygous (the majority) and homozygous-donor. The few off-diagonal lines are small, roughly symmetric disagreements at the detection margin, expected for two callers built on different reference variant sets.

title: Inv4m tagging-SNP genotype: 50K vs 200K RTIGER calls, ZEAL NIL panel
section: 2. Genotype frequency vs the single-locus BC2S3 expectation
text:
2. Genotype frequency vs the single-locus BC2S3 expectation






NoteBC2S3 Mendelian expectation at an unselected locus



Two backcrosses to B73 then three selfing generations. With no selection, donor-allele frequency is \(q = 1/8\) and heterozygosity \(1/4\) at BC2; three selfing generations shrink heterozygosity by \((1/2)^3\) while \(q\) is conserved:
\[
\Pr(Aa) = \tfrac14\left(\tfrac12\right)^3 = \tfrac{2}{64},\quad
\Pr(aa) = q - \tfrac12\Pr(Aa) = \tfrac{7}{64},\quad
\Pr(AA) = \tfrac{55}{64}.
\]
So an unselected BC2S3 locus is 85.9 % hom. recurrent, 3.1 % het, 10.9 % hom. donor, i.e. 14.1 % donor-carrying. This single-locus expectation is the null both call sets are tested against.




Show code
p_AA <- 55 / 64
p_Aa <- 2 / 64
p_aa <- 7 / 64
exp2 <- c(recurrent = p_AA, donor = p_Aa + p_aa)
exp3 <- c(REF = p_AA, HET = p_Aa, ALT = p_aa)

N <- nrow(byNIL)
o250 <- c(recurrent = byNIL[g250 == "recurrent", .N], donor = byNIL[g250 == "donor", .N])
o50 <- c(recurrent = byNIL[g50 == "recurrent", .N], donor = byNIL[g50 == "donor", .N])
o50_3 <- c(REF = byNIL[g50_3 == "REF", .N], HET = byNIL[g50_3 == "HET", .N], ALT = byNIL[g50_3 == "ALT", .N])



Stacked bar, both call sets alongside the BC2S3 expectation
Genotype-frequency spectrum in the REF / HET / ALT categories for all three groups. The 200K set does not resolve zygosity, so its Introgression calls are shown as homozygous ALT by assumption (it emits no heterozygotes); the 50K set carries its measured het / hom split.


Show code
o250_3 <- c(REF = unname(o250["recurrent"]), HET = 0, ALT = unname(o250["donor"])) # 200K donor := hom ALT
bar3 <- rbindlist(list(
  data.table(source = "200K\n(previous)", geno = names(o250_3), freq = o250_3 / N),
  data.table(source = "50K\n(current)", geno = names(o50_3), freq = o50_3 / N),
  data.table(source = "BC2S3\nexpected", geno = names(exp3), freq = exp3)
))
bar3[, geno := factor(geno, c("REF", "HET", "ALT"))]
bar3[, source := factor(source, c("200K\n(previous)", "50K\n(current)", "BC2S3\nexpected"))]

# donor-carrying total (HET + ALT) labelled atop each bar
donor_lab <- bar3[geno %in% c("HET", "ALT"), .(freq = sum(freq)), by = source]

ggplot(bar3, aes(source, freq, fill = geno)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72, color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(
    data = donor_lab, aes(x = source, y = freq, label = percent(freq, accuracy = 0.1)),
    vjust = -0.4, size = 4, fontface = "bold", inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = geno3, name = "Genotype",
    labels = c(REF = "hom. recurrent", HET = "heterozygous", ALT = "hom. donor")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  labs(
    x = NULL, y = "Fraction of NIL lines",
    title = "Genotype-frequency spectrum at the Inv4m tagging SNP",
    subtitle = sprintf("n = %d NILs (both calls) · label = donor-carrying %% (HET + ALT) · BC2S3 donor = 14.1%%", N)
  ) +
  theme_classic2(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "bottom")











χ² goodness-of-fit tests


Show code
gof <- function(obs, p, label) {
  tt <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(x = obs, p = p))
  data.table(
    comparison = label, classes = length(obs),
    X2 = unname(tt$statistic), df = unname(tt$parameter), p_value = tt$p.value
  )
}
rbindlist(list(
  gof(o250, exp2, "200K (previous) vs BC2S3   (2-class)"),
  gof(o50, exp2, "50K (current)   vs BC2S3   (2-class)"),
  gof(o50_3, exp3, "50K (current)   vs BC2S3   (3-class)")
)) |>
  mutate(p_value = format.pval(p_value, digits = 3, eps = 1e-300)) |>
  kable(digits = 1, caption = "χ² goodness-of-fit of each genotype spectrum against the single-locus BC2S3 expectation")

Reading it. On donor presence both call sets sit just below the 14.1 % BC2S3 expectation and track each other closely (200K ≈ 11.5 %, 50K ≈ 12.2 %); the 50K set is not significantly different from Mendelian expectation, the 200K only mildly so. The 3-class test is strongly rejected because the 50K carriers skew heterozygous (HET excess, hom-donor deficit vs three generations of selfing), consistent with introgressions maintained heterozygous rather than selfed to fixation, plus RTIGER’s tendency to call low-coverage / boundary donor tracts as heterozygous at ~0.4× skim.

title: Inv4m tagging-SNP genotype: 50K vs 200K RTIGER calls, ZEAL NIL panel
section: 3. Does the flowering effect change? (LMM refit)
text:
3. Does the flowering effect change? (LMM refit)
The paper’s model, refit with each genotype source. Both apply the paper’s both-arms-at-BC1 family filter on their own genotype definition.


Show code
fit_trait <- function(dd, trait, geno_col) {
  dd <- copy(dd)
  dd[, Inv4m := factor(get(geno_col), levels = c("recurrent", "donor"))]
  dd[, bc1 := substr(NIL, 1, 13)]
  dd[, bc2 := substr(NIL, 1, 16)]
  arms <- dd[!is.na(Inv4m), uniqueN(Inv4m), by = bc1]
  dd <- dd[bc1 %in% arms[V1 == 2, bc1] & !is.na(Inv4m) & !is.na(get(trait))]
  m <- suppressMessages(lmerTest::lmer(
    as.formula(paste(trait, "~ Inv4m + (1 | ancestry/bc2/NIL)")),
    data = dd
  ))
  fe <- summary(m)$coefficients["Inv4mdonor", ]
  data.table(
    genotype = geno_col, trait = trait,
    n_plots = nrow(dd), n_bc1_families = uniqueN(dd$bc1),
    est = fe["Estimate"], se = fe["Std. Error"], p = fe["Pr(>|t|)"]
  )
}
TRAITS <- c("DTA_corr", "DTS_corr", "PH_corr")
lmm_tbl <- rbindlist(lapply(TRAITS, function(tr) {
  rbindlist(list(
    fit_trait(pheno[!is.na(g250)], tr, "g250"),
    fit_trait(pheno[!is.na(g50)], tr, "g50")
  ))
}))
lmm_tbl[, genotype := factor(genotype, c("g250", "g50"), c("200K (previous)", "50K (current)"))]
lmm_tbl[order(trait, genotype)] |>
  kable(
    digits = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 3, 3, 6),
    col.names = c(
      "Genotype", "Trait", "n plots", "n BC1 families",
      "Inv4m effect (d/cm)", "SE", "p"
    ),
    caption = "Inv4m (donor − recurrent) fixed effect: 200K vs 50K genotype"

title: Inv4m tagging-SNP genotype: 50K vs 200K RTIGER calls, ZEAL NIL panel
text:
Takeaways

Concordance (confusion matrix). The 200K (previous) and 50K (current) RTIGER call sets agree on donor presence at the tagging SNP for ~98 % of lines; the 50K set additionally resolves the 200K Introgression state into heterozygous (majority) and homozygous-donor.
BC2S3 fit. Both sets recover a donor-carrying frequency (≈11,12 %) close to the single-locus BC2S3 expectation (14.1 %); the 50K set is statistically indistinguishable from it on presence. The 3-class spectrum departs from the strict selfing null through a heterozygote excess.
Flowering effect (LMM). The donor → earlier-flowering effect (DTS, DTA) is stable in direction and magnitude across both genotype sets; plant height stays non-significant. The paper’s conclusion does not depend on which RTIGER marker set defines the genotype.

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. The days-to-silk (DTS) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-snp50k, built from the same real BZea pipeline: field DTS → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same 1,403 near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa (Zx 578, Zv 385, Zd 255, Zl 118, Zh 61). The DTS BLUE is the per-field SpATS mean across two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5); union mean ≈ 82 ± 4 days. DTS is a flowering trait, essentially the female counterpart of DTA (anthesis): the two are tightly genetically correlated, so the candidate genes and the ancestry-flowering confound below are identical to the DTA notebook — only the phenotype changes.
As in DTA, K = teosinte-ancestry relatedness is collinear with the flowering QTL, so the mixed model absorbs the very signal it protects — the flowering candidates (ZmCCT10, ZCN8) are suppressed under MLM. The DTS QTL are therefore the province of JLM (nested-in-family stepwise), not the MLM Manhattan.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/dts_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/dts_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "DTS"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize flowering candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). The same v5 flowering candidates as DTA (these are maize genes; teosinte introgression at them drives flowering time in both sexes).


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/dts_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("DTS(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], QTL = gg$qtl[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("DTS candidates (B73 v5): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: OLS → MLM: the flowering confound and structure control
text:
OLS → MLM: the flowering confound and structure control
The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD); K deflates λ toward 1 and collapses the flowering peaks (ZmCCT10), the ancestry-QTL confound.


Show code
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea DTS — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_ols,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/dts_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)











Show code
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n > perm 5%` = c(nsig(ols$P, thr_ols), nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan's 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.")

section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated but catches sharp SNP hits (ZCN8) the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("DTS — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)

  sprintf("DTS — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Multi-caller DTS scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller DTS scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same DTS MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth. Caller reliability at low coverage: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER per-donor segments; binhmm 1 Mb bins) recover the correct teosinte fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the fresh per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and give λ-deflated scans. Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (DTS) changes.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
tb1 <- fread(here("data/teonam/dts_candidate_genes.tsv"))[symbol == "zmcct10"]
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  z <- sc[CHR == tb1$chr & abs(BP - tb1$start) <= 5e5]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `zmcct10 -log10P` = if (nrow(z)) round(max(z[is.finite(P) & P > 0, -log10(P)]), 2) else NA_real_
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "DTS MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the zmcct10 window-max -log10P. Aggregated callers (RTIGER, binhmm) recover teosinte presence ~0.10; per-SNP (control) and per-SNP recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and deflate λ_GC.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Ear-Number (EN) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. The ear-number (EN) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-eh-mlm-snp50k, built from the same real BZea pipeline end-to-end: field EN → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa. EN is ear number — the count of ears borne per plant — and the per-genotype value is the per-field SpATS mean across the two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5). Only the phenotype and the candidate panel change vs the EH notebook. A closely related prolificacy (Prolif) notebook shares this candidate panel.
Trait context. Ear number / prolificacy is a domestication-and-inflorescence-architecture trait: teosinte is highly branched and prolific (many small ears on long lateral branches), while B73 has strong apical dominance and typically one or two ears. The trait is therefore expected to respond strongly to teosinte introgression. The candidate panel is the curated maize ear-number / prolificacy cloned-gene set, split into two tiers: the core ear-number regulators — gt1 (grassy tillers1; HD-ZIP I TF underlying the prol1.1 QTL, represses axillary ear-bud growth, teosinte allele → prolific ears), tb1 (teosinte branched1; the classic domestication TCP TF whose Hopscotch insertion drives apical dominance and reduced ear number), and tru1 (tassels replace upper ears1; a direct tb1 target promoting ear-vs-tassel identity) — and a second tier of inflorescence-architecture / meristem / kernel-row-number genes (ub2/ub3 SBP-box branching, ra1/ra2/ra3 ramosa branch determinacy, fea2/fea3/td1 CLV–WUS meristem-size receptors). Because teosinte ancestry is broadly ear-number-increasing and partly collinear with K, the mixed model absorbs ancestry-correlated variance, and the model-independent JLM (nested-in-family stepwise) is the primary QTL tool — with gt1/tb1 the two loci most likely to surface as headline, effect-bearing QTL rather than polygenic background.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/en_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/en_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/en_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/en_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "EN"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ZEAL/BZea Ear-Number (EN) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize ear-number / prolificacy candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). Candidates are the curated cloned ear-number / prolificacy panel (core gt1/tb1/tru1 + inflorescence-architecture branching/meristem/KRN genes; v5 coordinates); pathway records the tier and protein/role each acts in.


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/en_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("EN(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("EN candidates (B73 v5; curated maize ear-number / prolificacy panel): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)

title: ZEAL/BZea Ear-Number (EN) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
OLS → MLM: structure control
The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD, unfixable by any fixed factor); K deflates λ toward 1 and removes the ancestry-correlated bulk that a near-isogenic, teosinte-ancestry-structured ear-number panel carries.


Show code
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea EN — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_ols,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/en_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)









The OLS Manhattan (no K) is genome-wide inflated (ancestry-block LD) — the same structure artifact the MLM K term removes below.


Show code
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n > perm 5%` = c(nsig(ols$P, thr_ols), nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan's 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Ear-Number (EN) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)
EN QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, entry by a 1000-permutation empirical α = 0.05 (Chen-style genome-wide FWER; per-trait)). JLM QTL are selected by the stepwise enter/exit P (a model-selection gate), independent of the per-scan permutation FWER line used for the GWAS Manhattans below. Model-independent — the primary EN analysis, as in Chen 2019.


Show code
p_lolli <- plot_lollipop(
  here("results/sim/zeal/tassel/en_jlm_native1.txt"), mlm,
  "ZEAL/BZea EN — JLM QTL genomic distribution (nested in taxon)",
  overlap_csv = overlap,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/en_jlm_lollipop_snp50k.png")
)
p_lolli

title: ZEAL/BZea Ear-Number (EN) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated — the 70%-missing per-SNP panel at 0.4× is sparse — but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing, and the demonstration that ancestry inference is mandatory at low coverage — identical in mechanism to the DTA/PH/EH notebooks.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("EN — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("EN — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/en_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

title: ZEAL/BZea Ear-Number (EN) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Multi-caller EN scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller EN scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same EN MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth (not a coverage sweep). The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below). Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (EN) changes.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/en_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
# best (max -log10P) EN candidate window per caller
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/en_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "EN MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the EN ear-number / prolificacy candidate windows.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaves-Above-Ear (LAE) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. The leaves-above-ear (LAE) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-en-mlm-snp50k, built from the same real BZea pipeline end-to-end: field LAE → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa. LAE is the number of leaves above the uppermost ear — a node/leaf-number count (B73 ≈ 5.7). It was scored CLY25 only, so the per-genotype value is the single-field SpATS BLUE. Only the phenotype and the candidate change vs the EN notebook.
Trait context. Leaf number above the ear is a vegetative-architecture / node-number trait, set by the number of phytomers the shoot apical meristem produces above the ear-bearing node and tightly linked to flowering time and total leaf number. The single a-priori candidate here is tu1 (tunicate1 / ZMM19) — the classical pod-corn MADS-box transcription factor (dominant Tu elongates the glumes so they enclose the kernel), which the cited study (Nat Commun 2024, doi:10.1038/s41467-024-54148-7) implicates in leaf/node number. tu1 sits on chr 4 at ~183.9 Mb (v5). As in DTA/EN, teosinte ancestry is broadly node-number-shifting and partly collinear with K, so the mixed model absorbs ancestry-correlated variance and the model-independent JLM (nested-in-family stepwise) is the primary QTL tool.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/lae_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/lae_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/lae_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/lae_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "LAE"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaves-Above-Ear (LAE) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate gene: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate gene: closest marker vs window-max marker
For the LAE candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C).


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/lae_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("LAE(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("LAE candidate (B73 v5; tu1/tunicate1): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaves-Above-Ear (LAE) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
OLS → MLM: structure control
The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD, unfixable by any fixed factor); K deflates λ toward 1 and removes the ancestry-correlated bulk that a near-isogenic, teosinte-ancestry-structured node-number panel carries.


Show code
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea LAE — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_ols,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/lae_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)









The OLS Manhattan (no K) is genome-wide inflated (ancestry-block LD) — the same structure artifact the MLM K term removes below.


Show code

  `n > perm 5%` = c(nsig(ols$P, thr_ols), nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaves-Above-Ear (LAE) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)
LAE QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, entry by a 1000-permutation empirical α = 0.05 (Chen-style genome-wide FWER; per-trait)). JLM QTL are selected by the stepwise enter/exit P (a model-selection gate), independent of the per-scan permutation FWER line used for the GWAS Manhattans below. Model-independent — the primary LAE analysis, as in Chen 2019.


Show code
p_lolli <- plot_lollipop(
  here("results/sim/zeal/tassel/lae_jlm_native1.txt"), mlm,
  "ZEAL/BZea LAE — JLM QTL genomic distribution (nested in taxon)",
  overlap_csv = overlap,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/lae_jlm_lollipop_snp50k.png")
)
p_lolli

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaves-Above-Ear (LAE) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated — the 70%-missing per-SNP panel at 0.4× is sparse — but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing, and the demonstration that ancestry inference is mandatory at low coverage — identical in mechanism to the DTA/PH/EH/EN notebooks.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("LAE — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("LAE — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/lae_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaves-Above-Ear (LAE) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Multi-caller LAE scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller LAE scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same LAE MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth (not a coverage sweep). The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below). Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (LAE) changes.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/lae_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
# best (max -log10P) LAE candidate window per caller
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/lae_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "LAE MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the max -log10P at the tu1 candidate window.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Plant-Height (PH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. The plant-height (PH) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-snp50k, built from the same real BZea pipeline end-to-end: field PH → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same 1,403 near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa (Zx mexicana 578, Zv parviglumis 385, Zd diploperennis 255, Zl luxurians 118, Zh huehuetenangensis 61). The PH BLUE is the per-field SpATS mean across two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5); union mean ≈ 183 ± 16 cm.
Trait context. Plant height is an agronomic/architecture trait, not a flowering trait. In TeoNAM (Chen 2019), PLHT is polygenic with only a few QTL of small effect and no single large-effect locus — the opposite of the DTA/flowering architecture that had ZmCCT10/ZCN8. The height candidates here are therefore the classic maize dwarf / gibberellin (GA) / brassinosteroid (BR) / auxin genes named in Peiffer et al. 2014, The Genetic Architecture of Maize Height (Genetics 196:1337) — br1, br2 (internode/auxin), an1, d1, d3, d9, gid1 (GA), brd1, na2 (BR), d8 (DELLA/GA signaling). Teosinte is taller and more branched than B73, so teosinte ancestry is broadly height-increasing and partly collinear with K; as in DTA, the mixed model absorbs ancestry-correlated variance, and the model-independent JLM (nested-in-family stepwise) is the primary QTL tool.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/ph_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/ph_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/ph_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/ph_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "PH"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ZEAL/BZea Plant-Height (PH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize plant-height candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). Candidates are the dwarf/GA/BR/auxin height genes from Peiffer et al. 2014 (v5 coordinates); pathway records the hormone/growth pathway each acts in.


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/ph_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("PH(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("PH candidates (B73 v5, Peiffer et al. 2014): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)
