title: ZEAL/BZea Plant-Height (PH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated — the 70%-missing per-SNP panel at 0.4× is sparse — but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing, and the demonstration that ancestry inference is mandatory at low coverage — identical in mechanism to the DTA notebook.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("PH — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("PH — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/ph_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

title: ZEAL/BZea Plant-Height (PH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Multi-caller PH scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller PH scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same PH MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth (not a coverage sweep). The scientific content is caller reliability at low coverage. The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the two block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER per-donor segments 0.111; binhmm 1 Mb bins 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the fresh per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation, by contrast, is phenotype-dependent: for PH the per-SNP callers stay λ-deflated (control 0.79, LB-Impute 0.85), the RTIGER mosaic is the best-calibrated (λ ≈ 1.3), and binhmm actually runs inflated (λ ≈ 2.9, many markers past its permutation FWER line) — a caution that recovering the right ancestry fraction does not by itself guarantee a well-calibrated scan when residual ancestry structure aligns with a polygenic trait like height. The caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (PH) changes vs the DTA notebook.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/ph_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
# best (max -log10P) PH candidate window per caller — PH is polygenic, no single headline gene
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/ph_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "PH MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the PH candidate windows. Aggregated callers (RTIGER, binhmm) recover teosinte presence ~0.10; per-SNP (control) and per-SNP recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and deflate λ_GC.")

Aggregated callers (RTIGER, binhmm) recover teosinte presence ~0.10; per-SNP (control) and per-SNP recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and deflate λ_GC.

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaf-Greenness (SPAD) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. The leaf-greenness (SPAD) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-ph-mlm-snp50k, built from the same real BZea pipeline end-to-end: field SPAD → SpATS spatial BLUE → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. SPAD is the leaf greenness index read by a SPAD meter on one leaf above the primary ear node — a proxy for leaf chlorophyll content. It was scored in CLY25 only, so the BLUE is a single-field SpATS mean (no cross-field union). Only the phenotype changes vs the PH/EH notebooks.
Trait context. SPAD is a physiological / leaf-chlorophyll trait, not an architecture or flowering trait. The candidate set spans the maize chlorophyll pathway and its regulators: chlorophyll biosynthesis (chlh1, oy1 = oil yellow1/Mg-chelatase I — the classic maize SPAD gene, gtr3, urod, por1, por2), chlorophyll catabolism / stay-green (pph, nye1/nye2/sgrl1 = SGR/STAY-GREEN, lls1 = lethal leaf spot1/PaO, nyc1 = chlorophyll b reductase), the light-harvesting antenna (lhcb10, cab1, cab2), and greenness regulators (nactf132/ZmNAC132, and the GOLDEN2-LIKE TFs g2, glk1). As in the other traits, teosinte ancestry is structured and partly collinear with K, so the model-independent JLM (nested-in-family stepwise) is the primary QTL tool and the MLM absorbs ancestry-correlated variance.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/spad_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/spad_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/spad_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/spad_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "SPAD"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaf-Greenness (SPAD) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize chlorophyll/greenness candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). pathway records the chlorophyll-pathway role (biosynthesis / catabolism-stay-green / light-harvesting / regulator).


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/spad_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("SPAD candidates (B73 v5; maize chlorophyll biosynthesis / catabolism / light-harvesting / regulators): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)

OLS → MLM: structure control

Show code

  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/spad_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)

  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),

  `n > perm 5%` = c(nsig(ols$P, thr_ols), nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan's 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaf-Greenness (SPAD) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("SPAD — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("SPAD — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaf-Greenness (SPAD) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Multi-caller SPAD scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller SPAD scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same SPAD MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth. The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below). Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (SPAD) changes.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/spad_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/spad_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "SPAD MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the chlorophyll candidate windows.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaf-Greenness SPAD (36 DAS) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. One of three SPAD notebooks. SPAD leaf greenness was measured at two dates (20 and 36 days after sowing). This notebook maps the 36DAS reading; its siblings map the 36DAS reading (spad36das) and a single spatially-corrected SPAD BLUE (spad). Same SNP50K ancestry pipeline → RTIGER mosaic → K → JLM + MLM.
Trait context. SPAD is a leaf-chlorophyll proxy. The candidate set spans the maize chlorophyll pathway and its regulators: biosynthesis (chlh1, oy1, gtr3, urod, por1/2), catabolism / stay-green (pph, nye1/2, sgrl1, lls1, nyc1), light-harvesting (lhcb10, cab1/2), and regulators (nactf132, GOLDEN2-LIKE g2/glk1). The model-independent JLM is the primary QTL tool.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R"))
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/spad36das_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/spad36das_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/spad36das_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/spad36das_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "SPAD36DAS"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaf-Greenness SPAD (36 DAS) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/spad36das_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("SPAD (36DAS) candidates (B73 v5 maize chlorophyll panel): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)

title: ZEAL/BZea Leaf-Greenness SPAD (36 DAS) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Multi-caller SPAD 36DAS scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller SPAD 36DAS scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/spad36das_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/spad36das_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "SPAD 36DAS MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the chlorophyll candidate windows.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Stem-Pubescence (StPu) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
text:
What this is. The stem-pubescence (StPu) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-stpi-mlm-snp50k, built from the same real BZea pipeline: field StPu → per-genotype empirical logit → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa. StPu is a binary 0/1 macrohair-presence call per plot (pubescent stem / not); the per-genotype phenotype is the proportion of pubescent plots, k/n, and only 72 of 1,423 genotypes (~5%) are ever pubescent. Like StPi (and unlike the continuous DTA/DTS/PH), it is modeled on the logit scale, not with a SpATS spatial BLUE.
Trait context. Stem pubescence is a macrohair trait: macrohairs (MHs) are the prominent epidermal hairs that mark adult vegetative identity and have long been implicated in insect resistance. The major regulator is macrohairless1 (mhl1) on the long arm of chromosome 9 (Moose, Lauter & Carlson 2004): recessive mhl1 alleles eliminate leaf-blade macrohairs, and a mexicana-derived neomorphic allele extends macrohairs from the blade onto the sheath — the “hairy sheath” pubescence phenotype rare in maize inbreds but common in teosinte. mhl1 has never been cloned (no causal gene), so it is mapped as a QTL: the three-point map places it between glossy15 (gl15, proximal) and umc1120 (distal) on 9L. It falls inside inv9f, a ~3 Mb mexicana-introgression inversion (v4 chr9:108,615,836–111,785,557 → v5 chr9:110,501,231–113,605,354; Crow et al. 2021) that shows a steep mexicana-ancestry cline with elevation. In v5 the inversion sits between the flanking anchors gl15 (Zm00001eb387280, ~101.0 Mb, proximal — an AP2-like juvenile→adult regulator upstream of mhl1) and umc1120 (Zm00001eb392660, ~127.2 Mb) / bk2 (brittle stalk2, Zm00001eb393070, ~128.9 Mb) distally. The candidate here is therefore the mhl1/inv9f interval itself, not a single cloned gene. A second, cloned trichome candidate is OCL4 (Zm00001eb024680, chr1), an HD-ZIP IV transcription factor required for trichome patterning and anther development in maize (Vernoud et al. 2009).
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD

# Overlay the published macrohair-density QTL (B73v4 lifted to v5) as translucent red bands.
# Uses the same cumulative-position transformer the Manhattan/lollipop use, built from the SAME
# scan passed to the plot, so bands align exactly with the plotted points.
add_qmh_bands <- function(p, scan) {
  s <- as.data.frame(scan)
  s <- s[is.finite(s$P) & s$P > 0, c("SNP", "CHR", "BP", "P")]
  tr <- get_transformer(s)
  q <- fread(here("data/teonam/stpu_prior_mh_qtl.tsv"))
  q$xmin <- tr(q$chr, q$ci_lo_bp)
  q$xmax <- tr(q$chr, q$ci_hi_bp)
  band <- geom_rect(
    data = q, aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
    inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "#d62728", alpha = 0.10
  )
  p$layers <- c(band, p$layers) # draw beneath the points/line
  p
}
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/stpu_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/stpu_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/stpu_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/stpu_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "STPU"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ZEAL/BZea Stem-Pubescence (StPu) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Candidate loci: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate loci: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each StPu trichome/macrohair candidate (B73 v5) — the uncloned macrohair QTL mhl1 inside the inv9f inversion; the AP2 phase-change factor gl15 (proximal flank of mhl1); the HD-ZIP IV TF OCL4; the WOX3 homeobox genes wox3a (ZmWOX3A) / wox3b; the SIAMESE-related CDK inhibitor smr4 (ZmSMR4); and the SBP-box/SPL family (tga1, tsh4, ub2, ub3, and the peak-proximal sbp11/sbp22) — the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). pathway records the locus role.


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/stpu_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("StPu candidate (B73 v5): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker across the mhl1/inv9f interval, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)

title: ZEAL/BZea Stem-Pubescence (StPu) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Prior macrohair (MH) QTL overlay (B73 v4 → v5)
text:
Prior macrohair (MH) QTL overlay (B73 v4 → v5)
Published macrohair-density QTL (Perez-Limón et al. 2022, G3 12(3):jkab447; a B73×Palomero Toluqueño highland-maize mapping population) — peak, Bayes support interval, additive variance explained, and the analyses detecting each — lifted from B73 v4 to v5 and overlaid as red bands on the JLM and RTIGER-mosaic Manhattans below. None of the StPu permutation-FWER peaks fall inside a qMH interval; the one biologically coherent overlap is qMH9 ⊃ mhl1/inv9f (its Bayes interval spans most of 9L), and the chr8 StPu peak lies ~2.4 Mb proximal to the tight, high-variance qMH8.


Show code
qmh <- fread(here("data/teonam/stpu_prior_mh_qtl.tsv"))
sc9 <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
mhl_iv <- c(110501231, 113605354) # mhl1/inv9f interval (v5)
best_in <- function(ch, lo, hi) {
  w <- sc9[CHR == ch & BP >= lo & BP <= hi]
  if (nrow(w)) round(max(-log10(w$P)), 2) else NA_real_
}
tabq <- qmh[, .(
  qtl, chr,
  `peak v5 (Mb)` = round(peak_bp / 1e6, 2),
  `Bayes CI v5 (Mb)` = sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", ci_lo_bp / 1e6, ci_hi_bp / 1e6),
  `Var (%)` = var_pct, analysis,
  `max StPu −log10P in CI` = mapply(best_in, chr, ci_lo_bp, ci_hi_bp),
  `contains mhl1/inv9f` = ifelse(chr == 9 & ci_hi_bp >= mhl_iv[1] & ci_lo_bp <= mhl_iv[2], "yes", "no")
)]
knitr::kable(tabq, caption = "Prior macrohair-density QTL from Perez-Limón et al. 2022 (G3 12(3):jkab447), the B73×Palomero Toluqueño highland-maize mapping population (peak + Bayes support interval + additive variance % + detecting analyses; B73v4 lifted to v5). Red bands on the JLM/RTIGER Manhattans below mark these intervals; `max StPu −log10P in CI` is the best RTIGER-mosaic MLM signal inside each.")

Show code
cat(sprintf("%d markers clear the 5%% FWER line (-log10P = %.2f)\n", nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm), thr_mlm))


0 markers clear the 5% FWER line (-log10P = 4.59)

title: ZEAL/BZea Stem-Pubescence (StPu) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("StPu — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)
p_C <- add_qmh_bands(plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("StPu — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
), mlm)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/stpu_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

title: ZEAL/BZea Stem-Pubescence (StPu) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER
section: Multi-caller StPu scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller StPu scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same StPu MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth. The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below). Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (StPu) changes.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/stpu_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/stpu_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "StPu MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the trichome candidate windows.")

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
text:
What this is. The days-to-silking (DTS) sibling of zeal-rqtl-dta-lod-profile. Same pipeline (scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R with TRAIT=DTS): a bcsft (BC₂S₃) cross, Haley–Knott interval mapping, a 1000-permutation genome-wide threshold, airmine multi-peak detection, and per-family scans. Silking and anthesis (DTA) share the same flowering-time architecture, so the candidate panel is the same — the interest is whether the silking QTL colocalize with the known genes and how they differ from DTA.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(qtl)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

TRAIT <- "DTS"
trait_lc <- tolower(TRAIT)
PFX <- sprintf("zeal_%s", trait_lc)
RQ <- here("results/sim/zeal/rqtl")
stopifnot(dir.exists(RQ)) # run: TRAIT=DTS Rscript scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R

bp_of <- function(m) as.integer(sub("^S[0-9]+_", "", m))
ci_str <- function(lbp, hbp) sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", pmin(lbp, hbp) / 1e6, pmax(lbp, hbp) / 1e6)

# candidate genes (B73 v5) for this trait, placed on the TeoNAM cM axis
cand <- fread(here(sprintf("data/teonam/%s_candidate_genes.tsv", trait_lc)))
cand[, mid := (start + end) / 2]
.nat <- fread(here("data/teonam/teonam_v5_native.tsv"))
cand[, cm := mapply(function(c, b) {
  n <- .nat[chr_v5 == c & is.finite(cm)][order(pos_v5)]
  if (nrow(n) < 2) NA_real_ else approx(n$pos_v5, n$cm, xout = b, rule = 2)$y
}, chr, mid)]
# candidate(s) whose gene body falls inside a peak's bp CI
cand_in_ci <- function(ch, lo, hi) {
  s <- cand[chr == ch & mid >= min(lo, hi) & mid <= max(lo, hi), symbol]
  if (length(s)) paste(s, collapse = ", ") else "–"
}

load_scan <- function(tag) {
  one <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_scanone_%s.csv", PFX, tag)))
  perms <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_perms_%s.rds", PFX, tag)))
  th <- summary(perms, alpha = c(0.05, 0.10))
  list(one = one, thr5 = as.numeric(th[1]), thr10 = as.numeric(th[2]))
}
nc <- load_scan("nocovar")
tx <- load_scan("taxon")
cross <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_cross.rds", PFX)))
NMARK <- totmar(cross)
Show code
g <- pull.geno(cross)
meta <- data.table(
  metric = c(sprintf("individuals (%s)", TRAIT), "markers (TeoNAM JLM grid)", "chromosomes", "H / het", "B / teosinte"),
  value = c(
    sum(!is.na(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))), NMARK, nchr(cross),
    sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 2, na.rm = TRUE)), sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 3, na.rm = TRUE))
  )
)
knitr::kable(meta, caption = "Run composition. Markers = the TeoNAM JLM 0.1-cM grid; genotypes = ZEAL mosaic projected onto it.")

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
section: Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)
text:
Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)
Faceted by chromosome. Blue = scanone + taxon covariate; grey = no covariate; dashed = the 1000-permutation 5% thresholds. Dotted grey verticals mark the flowering-time candidate genes (v5, placed on the cM axis).


Show code
prof <- rbindlist(list(
  cbind(nc$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "no covariate"),
  cbind(tx$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "+ taxon")
))[is.finite(lod)]
prof[, model := factor(model, levels = c("no covariate", "+ taxon"))]
sig <- prof[model == "+ taxon" & lod >= tx$thr5][, .SD[which.max(lod)], by = chr]
pal <- c("no covariate" = "grey65", "+ taxon" = "#2c6fbb")
ytop <- max(prof$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
cl <- cand[chr %in% unique(prof$chr) & is.finite(cm)]
p <- ggplot(prof, aes(pos, lod, colour = model)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = nc$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["no covariate"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = tx$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["+ taxon"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_point(data = sig, colour = "red", size = 1.6) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop * 1.45) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — R/qtl (bcsft) scanone, Haley–Knott (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),
    subtitle = sprintf("%s markers · 1000-perm 5%% threshold: no-cov = %.2f, +taxon = %.2f · dotted = flowering candidates", format(NMARK, big.mark = ","), nc$thr5, tx$thr5)
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_lod_profile.png", PFX)), p, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
p

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
section: Significant peaks (taxon model, α = 0.05) — one per chromosome, with candidate colocalization
text:
Significant peaks (taxon model, α = 0.05) — one per chromosome, with candidate colocalization
R/qtl’s summary() reports one peak per chromosome. The 1.5-LOD CI is in v5 Mb; candidate in CI lists any flowering gene whose body falls inside.


Show code
cipk <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_peaks_ci_taxon.csv", PFX)))
if (nrow(cipk) == 0) {
  cat("No genome-wide significant peaks.")
} else {
  knitr::kable(
    cipk[order(chr, -lod), .(
      chr,
      `pos (cM)` = round(pos, 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker) / 1e6, 1),
      LOD = round(lod, 2), pval, marker,
      `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = ci_str(ci_left, ci_right),
      `candidate in CI` = mapply(cand_in_ci, chr, ci_left, ci_right)
    )],
    caption = "Genome-wide significant QTL (taxon model), 1.5-LOD CI in v5 Mb, with any flowering candidate inside."
  )
}

Multi-peak refinement (airmine refine_peaks)
Multiple linked QTL per chromosome, via Akima interpolation + pracma::findpeaks + interval-graph deconvolution (trough-merge to avoid over-splitting broad pericentromeric peaks). CIs are the real-marker 1.5-LOD support.


Show code
refined <- function(tag) {
  r <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_peaks_refined_%s.csv", PFX, tag)))
  if (nrow(r) == 0) {
    return(r)
  }
  r[order(chr, -lod), .(
    chr,
    `pos (cM)` = round(pos, 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker) / 1e6, 1),
    LOD = round(lod, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thresh, 2), marker,
    `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = ci_str(ci_left, ci_right),
    `candidate in CI` = mapply(cand_in_ci, chr, ci_left, ci_right)
  )]
}
rt <- refined("taxon")
if (nrow(rt) == 0) cat("No refined peaks.") else knitr::kable(rt, caption = sprintf("Refined multi-peak QTL, taxon model (%d QTL across %d chromosomes).", nrow(rt), uniqueN(rt$chr)))

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
text:
Per-taxon (family-resolved), additive-only with QC
Each taxon’s NILs scanned separately on the HMM expected dosage (per-family filters: fraction introgressed > 0.05 & ≥ 3 samples per present genotype class), with the family’s own 1000-perm 5% threshold.


Show code
bt <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_scanone_by_taxon.csv", PFX)))[is.finite(lod)]
pal5 <- c(Zd = "#E41A1C", Zh = "#377EB8", Zl = "#4DAF4A", Zv = "#984EA3", Zx = "#FF7F00")
ytop_bt <- max(bt$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
pt <- ggplot(bt, aes(pos, lod, colour = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop_bt * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop_bt * 1.45) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(data = unique(bt[, .(taxon, thr5)]), aes(yintercept = thr5, colour = taxon), linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, name = "taxon") +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD (additive)",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — per-taxon additive scan (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),
    subtitle = "HMM expected dosage; per-family filters (frac introgressed > 0.05 & ≥3 per present class); dotted = flowering candidates"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_lod_by_taxon.png", PFX)), pt, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
pt











Show code
btpk <- bt[,
  {
    i <- which.max(lod)
    ch <- chr[i]
    L <- lod[i]
    ins <- .SD[chr == ch][lod >= L - 1.5]
    lo <- min(bp_of(ins$marker))
    hi <- max(bp_of(ins$marker))
    .(
      chr = ch, `pos (cM)` = round(pos[i], 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker[i]) / 1e6, 1),
      marker = marker[i], `max LOD` = round(L, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thr5[1], 2),
      sig = fifelse(L > thr5[1], "yes", "no"),
      `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", lo / 1e6, hi / 1e6),
      `candidate in CI` = cand_in_ci(ch, lo, hi)
    )
  },
  by = taxon
][order(-`max LOD`)]

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
section: Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon peak, by taxon
text:
Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon peak, by taxon


Show code
top <- btpk[sig == "yes"][which.max(`max LOD`)]
mk <- top$marker
gm <- pull.geno(cross)[, mk]
yv <- as.numeric(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))
ids <- as.character(pull.pheno(cross, "id"))
ss <- fread(here("data/zeal/samplesheet_3way.csv"))[, .(pedigree, taxon)]
d <- data.table(
  taxon = ss[match(ids, pedigree), taxon],
  geno = factor(c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")[gm], levels = c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")), y = yv
)[!is.na(y) & !is.na(geno) & taxon %in% names(pal5)]
summ <- d[, .(mean = mean(y), se = sd(y) / sqrt(.N), n = .N), by = .(taxon, geno)]
shown <- summ[n >= 3]
ncand <- cand[chr == top$chr][which.min(abs(mid - bp_of(mk)))]
ef <- ggplot(d, aes(geno, y, colour = taxon)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.12, size = 0.5) +
  geom_line(data = shown, aes(y = mean, group = taxon), linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(data = shown, aes(y = mean), size = 2.2) +
  geom_errorbar(data = shown, aes(y = mean, ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.15) +
  geom_text(data = summ, aes(label = n), y = min(d$y), vjust = 0, size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~taxon, nrow = 1) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    x = sprintf("genotype at %s (chr%d ~%.1f Mb; nearest candidate %s ~%.1f Mb)", mk, top$chr, bp_of(mk) / 1e6, ncand$symbol, ncand$mid / 1e6),
    y = sprintf("%s (BLUE)", TRAIT),
    title = sprintf("Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon %s peak — %s, by taxon", TRAIT, mk),
    subtitle = "per-genotype mean ± SE (grey = individual lines; n at base); estimate drawn for classes with n ≥ 3"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_%s_effect.png", PFX, mk)), ef, width = 10, height = 4.2, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
ef









Read. Effect size (days) and statistical support (LOD/n) are separate. A/A = B73, A/B = het, B/B = homozygous teosinte. Colocalization with a flowering candidate (the “candidate in CI” columns) is suggestive, not proof — the CIs are multi-Mb at this mapping resolution.

title: ZEAL/BZea EN — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
text:
What this is. The EN R/qtl scan — a sibling of the DTA/DTS/PH notebooks. All ZEAL traits go through one joint run (scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R): a single bcsft (BC₂S₃) cross, one calc.genoprob, one Haley–Knott scanone, and one shared 1000-permutation null across the whole panel; this notebook reads the pre-computed zeal_en_* slices. Multi-peak detection and per-family scans as in the other R/qtl notebooks.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(qtl)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

TRAIT <- "EN"
trait_lc <- tolower(TRAIT)
PFX <- sprintf("zeal_%s", trait_lc)
RQ <- here("results/sim/zeal/rqtl")
stopifnot(dir.exists(RQ)) # run: Rscript scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R

bp_of <- function(m) as.integer(sub("^S[0-9]+_", "", m))
ci_str <- function(lbp, hbp) sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", pmin(lbp, hbp) / 1e6, pmax(lbp, hbp) / 1e6)

# candidate genes (B73 v5) for this trait, placed on the TeoNAM cM axis
cand <- fread(here(sprintf("data/teonam/%s_candidate_genes.tsv", trait_lc)))
cand[, mid := (start + end) / 2]
.nat <- fread(here("data/teonam/teonam_v5_native.tsv"))
cand[, cm := mapply(function(c, b) {
  n <- .nat[chr_v5 == c & is.finite(cm)][order(pos_v5)]
  if (nrow(n) < 2) NA_real_ else approx(n$pos_v5, n$cm, xout = b, rule = 2)$y
}, chr, mid)]
cand_in_ci <- function(ch, lo, hi) {
  s <- cand[chr == ch & mid >= min(lo, hi) & mid <= max(lo, hi), symbol]
  if (length(s)) paste(s, collapse = ", ") else "–"
}

load_scan <- function(tag) {
  one <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_scanone_%s.csv", PFX, tag)))
  perms <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_perms_%s.rds", PFX, tag)))
  th <- summary(perms, alpha = c(0.05, 0.10))
  list(one = one, thr5 = as.numeric(th[1]), thr10 = as.numeric(th[2]))
}
nc <- load_scan("nocovar")
tx <- load_scan("taxon")
cross <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_cross.rds", PFX)))
NMARK <- totmar(cross)
Show code
g <- pull.geno(cross)
meta <- data.table(
  metric = c(sprintf("individuals (%s)", TRAIT), "markers (TeoNAM JLM grid)", "chromosomes", "H / het", "B / teosinte"),
  value = c(
    sum(!is.na(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))), NMARK, nchr(cross),
    sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 2, na.rm = TRUE)), sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 3, na.rm = TRUE))
  )
)
knitr::kable(meta, caption = "Run composition. Markers = the TeoNAM JLM 0.1-cM grid; genotypes = ZEAL mosaic projected onto it.")

title: ZEAL/BZea EN — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
section: Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)
text:
Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)
Faceted by chromosome. Blue = scanone + taxon covariate; grey = no covariate; dashed = the 1000-permutation 5% thresholds. Dotted grey verticals mark the candidate genes (v5, on the cM axis).


Show code
prof <- rbindlist(list(
  cbind(nc$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "no covariate"),
  cbind(tx$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "+ taxon")
))[is.finite(lod)]
prof[, model := factor(model, levels = c("no covariate", "+ taxon"))]
sig <- prof[model == "+ taxon" & lod >= tx$thr5][, .SD[which.max(lod)], by = chr]
pal <- c("no covariate" = "grey65", "+ taxon" = "#2c6fbb")
ytop <- max(prof$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
cl <- cand[chr %in% unique(prof$chr) & is.finite(cm)]
p <- ggplot(prof, aes(pos, lod, colour = model)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = nc$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["no covariate"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = tx$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["+ taxon"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_point(data = sig, colour = "red", size = 1.6) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop * 1.45) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — R/qtl (bcsft) scanone, Haley–Knott (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),
    subtitle = sprintf("%s markers · 1000-perm 5%% threshold: no-cov = %.2f, +taxon = %.2f · dotted = candidate genes", format(NMARK, big.mark = ","), nc$thr5, tx$thr5)
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_lod_profile.png", PFX)), p, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
p

text:
Per-taxon (family-resolved), additive-only with QC


Show code
bt <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_scanone_by_taxon.csv", PFX)))[is.finite(lod)]
pal5 <- c(Zd = "#E41A1C", Zh = "#377EB8", Zl = "#4DAF4A", Zv = "#984EA3", Zx = "#FF7F00")
ytop_bt <- max(bt$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
pt <- ggplot(bt, aes(pos, lod, colour = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop_bt * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop_bt * 1.45) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(data = unique(bt[, .(taxon, thr5)]), aes(yintercept = thr5, colour = taxon), linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, name = "taxon") +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD (additive)",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — per-taxon additive scan (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),
    subtitle = "HMM expected dosage; per-family filters (frac introgressed > 0.05 & ≥3 per present class); dotted = candidate genes"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_lod_by_taxon.png", PFX)), pt, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
pt











Show code
btpk <- bt[,
  {
    i <- which.max(lod)
    ch <- chr[i]
    L <- lod[i]
    ins <- .SD[chr == ch][lod >= L - 1.5]
    lo <- min(bp_of(ins$marker))
    hi <- max(bp_of(ins$marker))
    .(
      chr = ch, `pos (cM)` = round(pos[i], 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker[i]) / 1e6, 1),
      marker = marker[i], `max LOD` = round(L, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thr5[1], 2),
      sig = fifelse(L > thr5[1], "yes", "no"),
      `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", lo / 1e6, hi / 1e6),
      `candidate in CI` = cand_in_ci(ch, lo, hi)
    )
  },
  by = taxon
][order(-`max LOD`)]
knitr::kable(btpk, caption = "Genome-wide additive peak per taxon, with the per-taxon 1000-perm 5% threshold and any candidate gene in the CI. 'sig' = the family's peak exceeds its own threshold.")

    btr[order(taxon, chr, -lod), .(
      taxon, chr,
      `pos (cM)` = round(pos, 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker) / 1e6, 1),
      LOD = round(lod, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thr5, 2), marker,
      `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = ci_str(ci_left, ci_right),
      `candidate in CI` = mapply(cand_in_ci, chr, ci_left, ci_right)
    )],
    caption = sprintf("Refined per-taxon multi-peak QTL (%d QTL across %d families).", nrow(btr), uniqueN(btr$taxon))
  )
}

title: ZEAL/BZea EN — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
section: Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon peak, by taxon
text:
Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon peak, by taxon


Show code
if (nrow(btpk[sig == "yes"]) == 0) {
  cat("No per-taxon peak clears its threshold; no effect plot.")
} else {
  top <- btpk[sig == "yes"][which.max(`max LOD`)]
  mk <- top$marker
  gm <- pull.geno(cross)[, mk]
  yv <- as.numeric(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))
  ids <- as.character(pull.pheno(cross, "id"))
  ss <- fread(here("data/zeal/samplesheet_3way.csv"))[, .(pedigree, taxon)]
  d <- data.table(
    taxon = ss[match(ids, pedigree), taxon],
    geno = factor(c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")[gm], levels = c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")), y = yv
  )[!is.na(y) & !is.na(geno) & taxon %in% names(pal5)]
  summ <- d[, .(mean = mean(y), se = sd(y) / sqrt(.N), n = .N), by = .(taxon, geno)]
  shown <- summ[n >= 3]
  ncand <- cand[chr == top$chr][which.min(abs(mid - bp_of(mk)))]
  ncand_txt <- if (nrow(ncand)) sprintf("; nearest candidate %s ~%.1f Mb", ncand$symbol, ncand$mid / 1e6) else ""
  ef <- ggplot(d, aes(geno, y, colour = taxon)) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.12, size = 0.5) +
    geom_line(data = shown, aes(y = mean, group = taxon), linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(data = shown, aes(y = mean), size = 2.2) +
    geom_errorbar(data = shown, aes(y = mean, ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.15) +
    geom_text(data = summ, aes(label = n), y = min(d$y), vjust = 0, size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
    facet_wrap(~taxon, nrow = 1) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, guide = "none") +
    labs(
      x = sprintf("genotype at %s (chr%d ~%.1f Mb%s)", mk, top$chr, bp_of(mk) / 1e6, ncand_txt),
      y = sprintf("%s (BLUE)", TRAIT),
      title = sprintf("Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon %s peak — %s, by taxon", TRAIT, mk),
      subtitle = "per-genotype mean ± SE (grey = individual lines; n at base); estimate drawn for classes with n ≥ 3"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
  ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_%s_effect.png", PFX, mk)), ef, width = 10, height = 4.2, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
  print(ef)
}









Read. Effect size and statistical support (LOD/n) are separate. A/A = B73, A/B = het, B/B = homozygous teosinte. Colocalization with a candidate (the “candidate in CI” columns) is suggestive, not proof — the CIs are multi-Mb at this mapping resolution.

title: ZEAL/BZea LAE — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
text:
What this is. The LAE R/qtl scan — a sibling of the DTA/DTS/PH notebooks. All ZEAL traits go through one joint run (scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R): a single bcsft (BC₂S₃) cross, one calc.genoprob, one Haley–Knott scanone, and one shared 1000-permutation null across the whole panel; this notebook reads the pre-computed zeal_lae_* slices. Multi-peak detection and per-family scans as in the other R/qtl notebooks.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(qtl)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

TRAIT <- "LAE"
trait_lc <- tolower(TRAIT)
PFX <- sprintf("zeal_%s", trait_lc)
RQ <- here("results/sim/zeal/rqtl")
stopifnot(dir.exists(RQ)) # run: Rscript scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R

bp_of <- function(m) as.integer(sub("^S[0-9]+_", "", m))
ci_str <- function(lbp, hbp) sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", pmin(lbp, hbp) / 1e6, pmax(lbp, hbp) / 1e6)

# candidate genes (B73 v5) for this trait, placed on the TeoNAM cM axis
cand <- fread(here(sprintf("data/teonam/%s_candidate_genes.tsv", trait_lc)))
cand[, mid := (start + end) / 2]
.nat <- fread(here("data/teonam/teonam_v5_native.tsv"))
cand[, cm := mapply(function(c, b) {
  n <- .nat[chr_v5 == c & is.finite(cm)][order(pos_v5)]
  if (nrow(n) < 2) NA_real_ else approx(n$pos_v5, n$cm, xout = b, rule = 2)$y
}, chr, mid)]
cand_in_ci <- function(ch, lo, hi) {
  s <- cand[chr == ch & mid >= min(lo, hi) & mid <= max(lo, hi), symbol]
  if (length(s)) paste(s, collapse = ", ") else "–"
}

load_scan <- function(tag) {
  one <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_scanone_%s.csv", PFX, tag)))
  perms <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_perms_%s.rds", PFX, tag)))
  th <- summary(perms, alpha = c(0.05, 0.10))
  list(one = one, thr5 = as.numeric(th[1]), thr10 = as.numeric(th[2]))
}
nc <- load_scan("nocovar")
tx <- load_scan("taxon")
cross <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_cross.rds", PFX)))
NMARK <- totmar(cross)
Show code
g <- pull.geno(cross)
meta <- data.table(
  metric = c(sprintf("individuals (%s)", TRAIT), "markers (TeoNAM JLM grid)", "chromosomes", "H / het", "B / teosinte"),
  value = c(
    sum(!is.na(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))), NMARK, nchr(cross),
    sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 2, na.rm = TRUE)), sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 3, na.rm = TRUE))
  )
)
knitr::kable(meta, caption = "Run composition. Markers = the TeoNAM JLM 0.1-cM grid; genotypes = ZEAL mosaic projected onto it.")

title: ZEAL/BZea LAE — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
section: Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)
text:
Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)
Faceted by chromosome. Blue = scanone + taxon covariate; grey = no covariate; dashed = the 1000-permutation 5% thresholds. Dotted grey verticals mark the candidate genes (v5, on the cM axis).


Show code
prof <- rbindlist(list(
  cbind(nc$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "no covariate"),
  cbind(tx$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "+ taxon")
))[is.finite(lod)]
prof[, model := factor(model, levels = c("no covariate", "+ taxon"))]
sig <- prof[model == "+ taxon" & lod >= tx$thr5][, .SD[which.max(lod)], by = chr]
pal <- c("no covariate" = "grey65", "+ taxon" = "#2c6fbb")
ytop <- max(prof$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
cl <- cand[chr %in% unique(prof$chr) & is.finite(cm)]
p <- ggplot(prof, aes(pos, lod, colour = model)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = nc$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["no covariate"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = tx$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["+ taxon"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_point(data = sig, colour = "red", size = 1.6) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop * 1.45) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — R/qtl (bcsft) scanone, Haley–Knott (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),
    subtitle = sprintf("%s markers · 1000-perm 5%% threshold: no-cov = %.2f, +taxon = %.2f · dotted = candidate genes", format(NMARK, big.mark = ","), nc$thr5, tx$thr5)
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_lod_profile.png", PFX)), p, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
p

title: ZEAL/BZea LAE — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
text:
Per-taxon (family-resolved), additive-only with QC


Show code
bt <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_scanone_by_taxon.csv", PFX)))[is.finite(lod)]
pal5 <- c(Zd = "#E41A1C", Zh = "#377EB8", Zl = "#4DAF4A", Zv = "#984EA3", Zx = "#FF7F00")
ytop_bt <- max(bt$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
pt <- ggplot(bt, aes(pos, lod, colour = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop_bt * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop_bt * 1.45) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(data = unique(bt[, .(taxon, thr5)]), aes(yintercept = thr5, colour = taxon), linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, name = "taxon") +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD (additive)",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — per-taxon additive scan (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),
    subtitle = "HMM expected dosage; per-family filters (frac introgressed > 0.05 & ≥3 per present class); dotted = candidate genes"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_lod_by_taxon.png", PFX)), pt, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
pt











Show code
btpk <- bt[,
  {
    i <- which.max(lod)
    ch <- chr[i]
    L <- lod[i]
    ins <- .SD[chr == ch][lod >= L - 1.5]
    lo <- min(bp_of(ins$marker))
    hi <- max(bp_of(ins$marker))
    .(
      chr = ch, `pos (cM)` = round(pos[i], 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker[i]) / 1e6, 1),
      marker = marker[i], `max LOD` = round(L, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thr5[1], 2),
      sig = fifelse(L > thr5[1], "yes", "no"),
      `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", lo / 1e6, hi / 1e6),
      `candidate in CI` = cand_in_ci(ch, lo, hi)
    )
  },
  by = taxon
][order(-`max LOD`)]

title: ZEAL/BZea LAE — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
section: Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon peak, by taxon
text:
Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon peak, by taxon


Show code
if (nrow(btpk[sig == "yes"]) == 0) {
  cat("No per-taxon peak clears its threshold; no effect plot.")
} else {
  top <- btpk[sig == "yes"][which.max(`max LOD`)]
  mk <- top$marker
  gm <- pull.geno(cross)[, mk]
  yv <- as.numeric(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))
  ids <- as.character(pull.pheno(cross, "id"))
  ss <- fread(here("data/zeal/samplesheet_3way.csv"))[, .(pedigree, taxon)]
  d <- data.table(
    taxon = ss[match(ids, pedigree), taxon],
    geno = factor(c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")[gm], levels = c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")), y = yv
  )[!is.na(y) & !is.na(geno) & taxon %in% names(pal5)]
  summ <- d[, .(mean = mean(y), se = sd(y) / sqrt(.N), n = .N), by = .(taxon, geno)]
  shown <- summ[n >= 3]
  ncand <- cand[chr == top$chr][which.min(abs(mid - bp_of(mk)))]
  ncand_txt <- if (nrow(ncand)) sprintf("; nearest candidate %s ~%.1f Mb", ncand$symbol, ncand$mid / 1e6) else ""
  ef <- ggplot(d, aes(geno, y, colour = taxon)) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.12, size = 0.5) +
    geom_line(data = shown, aes(y = mean, group = taxon), linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(data = shown, aes(y = mean), size = 2.2) +
    geom_errorbar(data = shown, aes(y = mean, ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.15) +
    geom_text(data = summ, aes(label = n), y = min(d$y), vjust = 0, size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
    facet_wrap(~taxon, nrow = 1) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, guide = "none") +
    labs(
      x = sprintf("genotype at %s (chr%d ~%.1f Mb%s)", mk, top$chr, bp_of(mk) / 1e6, ncand_txt),
      y = sprintf("%s (BLUE)", TRAIT),
      title = sprintf("Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon %s peak — %s, by taxon", TRAIT, mk),
      subtitle = "per-genotype mean ± SE (grey = individual lines; n at base); estimate drawn for classes with n ≥ 3"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
  ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_%s_effect.png", PFX, mk)), ef, width = 10, height = 4.2, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
  print(ef)
}









Read. Effect size and statistical support (LOD/n) are separate. A/A = B73, A/B = het, B/B = homozygous teosinte. Colocalization with a candidate (the “candidate in CI” columns) is suggestive, not proof — the CIs are multi-Mb at this mapping resolution.

title: ZEAL/BZea PH — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
text:
What this is. The plant-height (PH) sibling of zeal-rqtl-dta-lod-profile. Same pipeline (scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R with TRAIT=PH): a bcsft (BC₂S₃) cross, Haley–Knott interval mapping, a 1000-permutation genome-wide threshold, airmine multi-peak detection, and per-family scans. Teosinte is taller/more branched than B73, so the candidate panel is the classic maize dwarf/gibberellin/brassinosteroid/auxin height genes — the question is whether the PH QTL colocalize with them.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(qtl)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

TRAIT <- "PH"
trait_lc <- tolower(TRAIT)
PFX <- sprintf("zeal_%s", trait_lc)
RQ <- here("results/sim/zeal/rqtl")
stopifnot(dir.exists(RQ)) # run: TRAIT=PH Rscript scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R

bp_of <- function(m) as.integer(sub("^S[0-9]+_", "", m))
ci_str <- function(lbp, hbp) sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", pmin(lbp, hbp) / 1e6, pmax(lbp, hbp) / 1e6)

# candidate genes (B73 v5) for this trait, placed on the TeoNAM cM axis
cand <- fread(here(sprintf("data/teonam/%s_candidate_genes.tsv", trait_lc)))
cand[, mid := (start + end) / 2]
.nat <- fread(here("data/teonam/teonam_v5_native.tsv"))
cand[, cm := mapply(function(c, b) {
  n <- .nat[chr_v5 == c & is.finite(cm)][order(pos_v5)]
  if (nrow(n) < 2) NA_real_ else approx(n$pos_v5, n$cm, xout = b, rule = 2)$y
}, chr, mid)]
# candidate(s) whose gene body falls inside a peak's bp CI
cand_in_ci <- function(ch, lo, hi) {
  s <- cand[chr == ch & mid >= min(lo, hi) & mid <= max(lo, hi), symbol]
  if (length(s)) paste(s, collapse = ", ") else "–"
}

load_scan <- function(tag) {
  one <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_scanone_%s.csv", PFX, tag)))
  perms <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_perms_%s.rds", PFX, tag)))
  th <- summary(perms, alpha = c(0.05, 0.10))
  list(one = one, thr5 = as.numeric(th[1]), thr10 = as.numeric(th[2]))
}
nc <- load_scan("nocovar")
tx <- load_scan("taxon")
cross <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_cross.rds", PFX)))
NMARK <- totmar(cross)
Show code
g <- pull.geno(cross)
meta <- data.table(
  metric = c(sprintf("individuals (%s)", TRAIT), "markers (TeoNAM JLM grid)", "chromosomes", "H / het", "B / teosinte"),
  value = c(
    sum(!is.na(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))), NMARK, nchr(cross),
    sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 2, na.rm = TRUE)), sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 3, na.rm = TRUE))
  )
)
knitr::kable(meta, caption = "Run composition. Markers = the TeoNAM JLM 0.1-cM grid; genotypes = ZEAL mosaic projected onto it.")

title: ZEAL/BZea PH — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
section: Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)
text:
Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)
Faceted by chromosome. Blue = scanone + taxon covariate; grey = no covariate; dashed = the 1000-permutation 5% thresholds. Dotted grey verticals mark the height candidate genes (v5, placed on the cM axis).


Show code
prof <- rbindlist(list(
  cbind(nc$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "no covariate"),
  cbind(tx$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "+ taxon")
))[is.finite(lod)]
prof[, model := factor(model, levels = c("no covariate", "+ taxon"))]
sig <- prof[model == "+ taxon" & lod >= tx$thr5][, .SD[which.max(lod)], by = chr]
pal <- c("no covariate" = "grey65", "+ taxon" = "#2c6fbb")
ytop <- max(prof$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
cl <- cand[chr %in% unique(prof$chr) & is.finite(cm)]
p <- ggplot(prof, aes(pos, lod, colour = model)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = nc$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["no covariate"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = tx$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["+ taxon"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_point(data = sig, colour = "red", size = 1.6) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop * 1.45) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — R/qtl (bcsft) scanone, Haley–Knott (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),
    subtitle = sprintf("%s markers · 1000-perm 5%% threshold: no-cov = %.2f, +taxon = %.2f · dotted = height candidates", format(NMARK, big.mark = ","), nc$thr5, tx$thr5)
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_lod_profile.png", PFX)), p, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
p

Significant peaks (taxon model, α = 0.05) — one per chromosome, with candidate colocalization
R/qtl’s summary() reports one peak per chromosome. The 1.5-LOD CI is in v5 Mb; candidate in CI lists any height gene whose body falls inside.


Show code

    )],

  )

Multi-peak refinement (airmine refine_peaks)
Multiple linked QTL per chromosome, via Akima interpolation + pracma::findpeaks + interval-graph deconvolution (trough-merge to avoid over-splitting broad pericentromeric peaks). CIs are the real-marker 1.5-LOD support.


Show code
refined <- function(tag) {
  r <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_peaks_refined_%s.csv", PFX, tag)))
  if (nrow(r) == 0) {
    return(r)
  }
  r[order(chr, -lod), .(
    chr,
    `pos (cM)` = round(pos, 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker) / 1e6, 1),
    LOD = round(lod, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thresh, 2), marker,
    `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = ci_str(ci_left, ci_right),
    `candidate in CI` = mapply(cand_in_ci, chr, ci_left, ci_right)
  )]
}

title: ZEAL/BZea PH — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
text:
Per-taxon (family-resolved), additive-only with QC
Each taxon’s NILs scanned separately on the HMM expected dosage (per-family filters: fraction introgressed > 0.05 & ≥ 3 samples per present genotype class), with the family’s own 1000-perm 5% threshold.


Show code
bt <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_scanone_by_taxon.csv", PFX)))[is.finite(lod)]
pal5 <- c(Zd = "#E41A1C", Zh = "#377EB8", Zl = "#4DAF4A", Zv = "#984EA3", Zx = "#FF7F00")
ytop_bt <- max(bt$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
pt <- ggplot(bt, aes(pos, lod, colour = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop_bt * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop_bt * 1.45) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(data = unique(bt[, .(taxon, thr5)]), aes(yintercept = thr5, colour = taxon), linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, name = "taxon") +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD (additive)",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — per-taxon additive scan (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),
    subtitle = "HMM expected dosage; per-family filters (frac introgressed > 0.05 & ≥3 per present class); dotted = height candidates"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_lod_by_taxon.png", PFX)), pt, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
pt











Show code
btpk <- bt[,
  {
    i <- which.max(lod)
    ch <- chr[i]
    L <- lod[i]
    ins <- .SD[chr == ch][lod >= L - 1.5]
    lo <- min(bp_of(ins$marker))
    hi <- max(bp_of(ins$marker))
    .(
      chr = ch, `pos (cM)` = round(pos[i], 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker[i]) / 1e6, 1),
      marker = marker[i], `max LOD` = round(L, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thr5[1], 2),
      sig = fifelse(L > thr5[1], "yes", "no"),
      `1.5-LOD CI (Mb)` = sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", lo / 1e6, hi / 1e6),
      `candidate in CI` = cand_in_ci(ch, lo, hi)
    )
  },
  by = taxon
][order(-`max LOD`)]

if (nrow(btr) == 0) {

title: ZEAL/BZea PH — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
section: Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon peak, by taxon
text:
Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon peak, by taxon


Show code
top <- btpk[sig == "yes"][which.max(`max LOD`)]
mk <- top$marker
gm <- pull.geno(cross)[, mk]
yv <- as.numeric(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))
ids <- as.character(pull.pheno(cross, "id"))
ss <- fread(here("data/zeal/samplesheet_3way.csv"))[, .(pedigree, taxon)]
d <- data.table(
  taxon = ss[match(ids, pedigree), taxon],
  geno = factor(c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")[gm], levels = c("A/A", "A/B", "B/B")), y = yv
)[!is.na(y) & !is.na(geno) & taxon %in% names(pal5)]
summ <- d[, .(mean = mean(y), se = sd(y) / sqrt(.N), n = .N), by = .(taxon, geno)]
shown <- summ[n >= 3]
ncand <- cand[chr == top$chr][which.min(abs(mid - bp_of(mk)))]
ef <- ggplot(d, aes(geno, y, colour = taxon)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.12, size = 0.5) +
  geom_line(data = shown, aes(y = mean, group = taxon), linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(data = shown, aes(y = mean), size = 2.2) +
  geom_errorbar(data = shown, aes(y = mean, ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.15) +
  geom_text(data = summ, aes(label = n), y = min(d$y), vjust = 0, size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~taxon, nrow = 1) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    x = sprintf("genotype at %s (chr%d ~%.1f Mb; nearest candidate %s ~%.1f Mb)", mk, top$chr, bp_of(mk) / 1e6, ncand$symbol, ncand$mid / 1e6),
    y = sprintf("%s (BLUE)", TRAIT),
    title = sprintf("Allele effect at the strongest per-taxon %s peak — %s, by taxon", TRAIT, mk),
    subtitle = "per-genotype mean ± SE (grey = individual lines; n at base); estimate drawn for classes with n ≥ 3"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_%s_effect.png", PFX, mk)), ef, width = 10, height = 4.2, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
ef









Read. Effect size and statistical support (LOD/n) are separate. A/A = B73, A/B = het, B/B = homozygous teosinte (teosinte alleles generally raise height). Colocalization with a height candidate (the “candidate in CI” columns) is suggestive, not proof — the CIs are multi-Mb at this mapping resolution.

title: ZEAL/BZea SPAD — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
text:
What this is. The SPAD R/qtl scan — a sibling of the DTA/DTS/PH notebooks. All ZEAL traits go through one joint run (scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R): a single bcsft (BC₂S₃) cross, one calc.genoprob, one Haley–Knott scanone, and one shared 1000-permutation null across the whole panel; this notebook reads the pre-computed zeal_spad_* slices. Multi-peak detection and per-family scans as in the other R/qtl notebooks.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(qtl)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

TRAIT <- "SPAD"
trait_lc <- tolower(TRAIT)
PFX <- sprintf("zeal_%s", trait_lc)
RQ <- here("results/sim/zeal/rqtl")
stopifnot(dir.exists(RQ)) # run: Rscript scripts/zeal_rqtl_scan.R

bp_of <- function(m) as.integer(sub("^S[0-9]+_", "", m))
ci_str <- function(lbp, hbp) sprintf("%.1f–%.1f", pmin(lbp, hbp) / 1e6, pmax(lbp, hbp) / 1e6)

# candidate genes (B73 v5) for this trait, placed on the TeoNAM cM axis
cand <- fread(here(sprintf("data/teonam/%s_candidate_genes.tsv", trait_lc)))
cand[, mid := (start + end) / 2]
.nat <- fread(here("data/teonam/teonam_v5_native.tsv"))
cand[, cm := mapply(function(c, b) {
  n <- .nat[chr_v5 == c & is.finite(cm)][order(pos_v5)]
  if (nrow(n) < 2) NA_real_ else approx(n$pos_v5, n$cm, xout = b, rule = 2)$y
}, chr, mid)]
cand_in_ci <- function(ch, lo, hi) {
  s <- cand[chr == ch & mid >= min(lo, hi) & mid <= max(lo, hi), symbol]
  if (length(s)) paste(s, collapse = ", ") else "–"
}

load_scan <- function(tag) {
  one <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_scanone_%s.csv", PFX, tag)))
  perms <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_perms_%s.rds", PFX, tag)))
  th <- summary(perms, alpha = c(0.05, 0.10))
  list(one = one, thr5 = as.numeric(th[1]), thr10 = as.numeric(th[2]))
}
nc <- load_scan("nocovar")
tx <- load_scan("taxon")
cross <- readRDS(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_cross.rds", PFX)))
NMARK <- totmar(cross)
Show code
g <- pull.geno(cross)
meta <- data.table(
  metric = c(sprintf("individuals (%s)", TRAIT), "markers (TeoNAM JLM grid)", "chromosomes", "H / het", "B / teosinte"),
  value = c(
    sum(!is.na(pull.pheno(cross, TRAIT))), NMARK, nchr(cross),
    sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 2, na.rm = TRUE)), sprintf("%.3f", mean(g == 3, na.rm = TRUE))
  )
)
knitr::kable(meta, caption = "Run composition. Markers = the TeoNAM JLM 0.1-cM grid; genotypes = ZEAL mosaic projected onto it.")

title: ZEAL/BZea SPAD — R/qtl (bcsft) on the TeoNAM JLM grid: LOD profile + per-family effects
section: Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)
text:
Genome-wide LOD profile (taxon model, with the no-covariate profile behind it)
Faceted by chromosome. Blue = scanone + taxon covariate; grey = no covariate; dashed = the 1000-permutation 5% thresholds. Dotted grey verticals mark the candidate genes (v5, on the cM axis).


Show code
prof <- rbindlist(list(
  cbind(nc$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "no covariate"),
  cbind(tx$one[, .(marker, chr, pos, lod)], model = "+ taxon")
))[is.finite(lod)]
prof[, model := factor(model, levels = c("no covariate", "+ taxon"))]
sig <- prof[model == "+ taxon" & lod >= tx$thr5][, .SD[which.max(lod)], by = chr]
pal <- c("no covariate" = "grey65", "+ taxon" = "#2c6fbb")
ytop <- max(prof$lod, na.rm = TRUE)
cl <- cand[chr %in% unique(prof$chr) & is.finite(cm)]
p <- ggplot(prof, aes(pos, lod, colour = model)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = nc$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["no covariate"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = tx$thr5, linetype = "dashed", colour = pal[["+ taxon"]], linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_point(data = sig, colour = "red", size = 1.6) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop * 1.45) +
  facet_grid(~chr, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x", switch = "x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +
  labs(
    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — R/qtl (bcsft) scanone, Haley–Knott (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),
    subtitle = sprintf("%s markers · 1000-perm 5%% threshold: no-cov = %.2f, +taxon = %.2f · dotted = candidate genes", format(NMARK, big.mark = ","), nc$thr5, tx$thr5)
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.05, "lines"), strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0))
ggsave(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_lod_profile.png", PFX)), p, width = 11, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
p

Show code

pt <- ggplot(bt, aes(pos, lod, colour = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(data = cl, aes(xintercept = cm), inherit.aes = FALSE, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = cl, aes(x = cm, y = ytop_bt * 1.18, label = symbol), inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, colour = "grey25", angle = 90, hjust = 0, direction = "x", segment.size = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = ytop_bt * 1.45) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(data = unique(bt[, .(taxon, thr5)]), aes(yintercept = thr5, colour = taxon), linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) +

  scale_colour_manual(values = pal5, name = "taxon") +

    x = "Chromosome (cM, TeoNAM JLM map)", y = "LOD (additive)",
    title = sprintf("ZEAL/BZea %s — per-taxon additive scan (TeoNAM JLM grid)", TRAIT),

      chr = ch, `pos (cM)` = round(pos[i], 1), `pos (Mb)` = round(bp_of(marker[i]) / 1e6, 1),
      marker = marker[i], `max LOD` = round(L, 2), `perm 5% thr` = round(thr5[1], 2),

    )
  },
  by = taxon
][order(-`max LOD`)]

btr <- fread(file.path(RQ, sprintf("%s_peaks_refined_by_taxon.csv", PFX)))
if (nrow(btr) == 0) {

    )],
    caption = sprintf("Refined per-taxon multi-peak QTL (%d QTL across %d families).", nrow(btr), uniqueN(btr$taxon))
  )
